The Dharwar rock system belongs to one of the oldest geological formations of India, dating back to the Precambrian
period. These rocks were originally sedimentary deposits, but over millions of years they were subjected to heat and
pressure inside the Earth, which transformed them into metamorphic rocks.
Because of these geological processes, the Dharwar rocks became rich repositories of metallic minerals. Many important
mineral deposits of India are associated with these rocks, including Iron ore, Manganese, Gold, Copper, Chromite and
others. This is why regions dominated by Dharwar rocks such as parts of Karnataka, Jharkhand, Odisha, and
Chhattisgarh, have historically been major mining and metallurgical centres.
Knowledge Base:
Rock System Key Feature
Dharwar Metallic minerals (iron, manganese, gold)
Vindhyan Sandstone, limestone, shale
Gondwana Coal deposits
Deccan Trap Basaltic lava flows

questions.text: More than three-fourths of world's Niobium, a critical mineral required for the variety of applications from superconductivity research to steel manufacturing, is produced by-
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
Niobium is a strategically important critical mineral used in several advanced industrial applications. It significantly
improves the strength, durability, and heat resistance of steel alloys, and is also used in superconducting materials,
aerospace components, electronics, nuclear technology and steel manufacturing. Because of its strategic and
technological importance, the global distribution of niobium production is highly concentrated.
Brazil is the world’s largest producer of Niobium, accounting for more than three-fourths (around 80–90%) of global
production. The country hosts some of the largest niobium reserves in the world, particularly in the Araxa and Catalao
deposits. Brazil’s dominance in niobium production makes it a critical player in global supply chains, especially for
industries that require high-strength steel alloys and superconducting materials.

questions.text: India has recently decided to establish her first ‘National Coral Reef Research Institute’ in-
questions.option_d: Andaman & Nicobar Islands dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
India possesses some of the most ecologically rich coral reef ecosystems in regions such as Lakshadweep, Gulf of
Mannar, Gulf of Kachchh, and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands. Coral reefs play a crucial role in marine biodiversity
conservation, coastal protection, fisheries productivity, and climate resilience.

[25]

The Government of India has recently decided to establish the country’s first ‘National Coral Reef Research Institute’ in
the Andaman & Nicobar Islands. The region contains some of the most diverse and well-developed coral reef
ecosystems in India, with high species richness and relatively healthier reef systems compared to many other regions.
The institute is expected to strengthen scientific research, reef monitoring, climate impact studies, and conservation
strategies, thereby supporting sustainable management of India’s coral reef resources.

questions.text: Which among the following countries has a border with the South China Sea?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
Brunei is a small Southeast Asian country located on the northern coast of the island of Borneo, and it directly faces
the South China Sea. Because of this geographical location, Brunei is one of the claimant states in the South China Sea
disputes, particularly regarding parts of the Spratly Islands and surrounding maritime zones.
Option a is incorrect. Japan is located to the northeast of the East China Sea and the Pacific Ocean. The country is
involved in maritime disputes with China over the Senkaku/Diaoyu Islands in the East China Sea, but Japan does not
border the South China Sea.
Option c is incorrect. North Korea does not have a border with the South China Sea.
Option d is incorrect. Laos is a landlocked country and does not have a border with the South China Sea.
Knowledge Base:

[26]

questions.text: Which of the following statements regarding the Tropical Thorn Forests in India are correct? I. They are found in semi-arid climatic conditions where the annual rainfall is generally below 70 cm. II. These forests are commonly found in Rajasthan, Gujarat, south-west Punjab and Haryana. III. Major plant species found in these forests include Babool, Ber and Khejri. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
India’s natural vegetation varies according to rainfall, temperature, and soil conditions. In areas where rainfall is
extremely low, plants evolve special adaptations to survive water scarcity and high evaporation. Such ecological
conditions give rise to Tropical Thorn Forests and Scrub vegetation.
Statement I is correct. Tropical Thorn Forests develop in semi-arid climatic regions where annual rainfall is generally
below about 70 cm. Due to such limited precipitation and high evaporation rates, only drought-resistant vegetation can
survive in these areas.
Statement II is correct. In India, these forests are mainly found in the north-western and central semi-arid belt,
including Rajasthan, Gujarat, south-west Punjab, Haryana, and parts of Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh. These
regions experience irregular rainfall and prolonged dry seasons, which favour the growth of thorny scrub vegetation.
Statement III is correct. The dominant plant species in Tropical Thorn Forests include babool (Acacia), ber, khair, Khejri,
neem, palas and wild date palm. These species are well adapted to dry climatic conditions and are commonly found
scattered across these semi-arid landscapes.
Plants growing in thorn forests show distinct structural adaptations to conserve water (xerophytic adaptations). They
usually possess deep root systems to tap underground moisture, thick or small leaves to minimise evaporation, and
sometimes succulent stems for water storage. These features help plants survive long dry periods and intense heat.

questions.text: Consider the following statements regarding salinity conditions in different seas: I. The North Sea records relatively low salinity because of its high-latitude location and significant freshwater inflow from rivers. II. The Arabian Sea records relatively high salinity because of high evaporation and comparatively limited freshwater inflow. III. The Caspian Sea records relatively high salinity because of intense evaporation and its landlocked nature with limited water exchange. Which of the above given statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
Salinity refers to the total concentration of dissolved salts present in water. It is measured as the amount of salt (in
grams) dissolved in 1,000 grams (1 kg) of water and is generally expressed in parts per thousand (‰ or ppt). Salinity is a
crucial property of seawater as it influences ocean circulation, marine ecosystems, and water density. Ocean salinity
varies across different seas and oceans depending on factors such as latitude, evaporation rates, freshwater inflow from
rivers, precipitation, and degree of enclosure from open oceans.
Statement I is incorrect. Although the North Sea lies in higher latitudes, it does not record low salinity. In fact, the
North Sea records relatively higher salinity because saline waters from the North Atlantic are brought into the sea by
the North Atlantic Drift. This inflow of saline Atlantic water offsets the effect of freshwater inflow from rivers and
maintains relatively higher salinity levels.
Statement II is correct. The Arabian Sea records relatively high salinity mainly because of high evaporation rates under
tropical climatic conditions. In addition, the freshwater inflow from rivers is comparatively limited, and evaporation
removes water but leaves salts behind, thereby increasing the salt concentration in the sea water.

[27]

Statement III is incorrect. The Caspian Sea does not record high salinity. Although it is landlocked, it receives
significant freshwater inflow from rivers, especially the Volga, which greatly reduces the salt concentration. As a result,
the salinity of the Caspian Sea is considerably lower than that of the open ocean.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Among the top ten economies of the world, India accounts for the highest percentage share of the services sector in its total Gross Value Added (GVA). Statement II: According to recent official statistics of the Government of India, the services sector has accounted for more than half of India’s total GVA in the last decade. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Statement I is incorrect: Among the major economies of the world (such as the United States, United Kingdom, Japan,
Germany, France, China, etc.), India does not account for the highest share of the services sector in its GVA. India’s
services sector contributes around 53–56% of GDP/GVA in recent years, which is significant for a developing economy
but lower than that of most advanced major economies. For instance:
● United States: ~78–80%
● United Kingdom: ~70–73%
● European Union: ~65–68%
● China: ~53–57%
Advanced economies generally have services shares in the 65–80% range due to structural transformation, whereas
India’s is still transitioning.
Statement II is correct: In India, the services sector contributes more than 50% of the country’s GVA in recent years,
according to official economic statistics. Its share has increased from about 50.6% in FY2014 to around 55% in FY 2025.
It is the dominant sector of the Indian economy, and includes activities such as trade, transport, communication,
financial services, information technology, tourism, and other professional services.
Hence, the correct answer is: c) Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: The China Plus One strategy adopted by several multinational corporations may create opportunities for India to expand its manufacturing sector and attract global supply chains. Statement II: Under the China Plus One strategy, companies seek to diversify their manufacturing and supply chains by establishing production facilities in countries other than China. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: The "China Plus One" (C+1) strategy has been increasingly adopted by multinational
corporations and several major economies to reduce excessive dependence on China for manufacturing.
● As companies seek alternative production destinations, countries such as India, Vietnam, Thailand, and Mexico are
emerging as potential manufacturing hubs.
● India, with its large domestic market, improving infrastructure, and initiatives such as Production Linked Incentive
(PLI) schemes and Make in India, can attract investments from firms relocating or expanding their manufacturing
bases.
Hence, this strategy can create opportunities for India to expand its manufacturing sector and integrate more deeply
into global supply chains.
Statement II is correct: The China Plus One strategy refers to a manufacturing and supply-chain diversification
strategy in which companies maintain manufacturing operations in China but simultaneously establish additional
production facilities in other countries.
The objective is to reduce supply-chain risks arising from excessive reliance on a single country, especially in the context
of rising labour costs in China, trade tensions, geopolitical risks, and disruptions such as the COVID-19 pandemic.
Statement II explains Statement I:
Since companies are diversifying their manufacturing bases and supply chains beyond China, they are looking for
alternative locations for production. This diversification creates opportunities for countries like India to attract
manufacturing investments and become part of global supply chains. Therefore, the supply-chain diversification
described in Statement II directly explains why India may benefit as mentioned in Statement I.
Thus, Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Regenerative agriculture techniques can serve as a crucial strategy to ensure food security while simultaneously addressing challenges posed by climate change. Statement II: Regenerative farming practices such as crop diversification, cover cropping and reduced tillage improve soil health, enhance water retention and increase carbon sequestration. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Regenerative agriculture refers to a set of farming practices aimed at restoring and enhancing the health of soil,
ecosystems, and biodiversity while maintaining sustainable agricultural productivity.
● Unlike conventional agriculture, which often focuses mainly on maximizing yields by relying heavily on chemical
inputs, regenerative agriculture emphasizes improving soil fertility, increasing organic matter, and strengthening
natural ecological processes.
● It promotes practices such as crop diversification, cover cropping, reduced or no tillage, agroforestry, and
integrated livestock management. These practices help improve soil structure, enhance water retention, reduce
soil erosion, and increase soil carbon sequestration.
● By restoring degraded soils and improving the resilience of farming systems, regenerative agriculture can contribute
to sustainable food production, climate change mitigation through carbon sequestration, and greater resilience of
agriculture to climate-related stresses such as droughts and floods.

[29]

Statement I is correct: Regenerative agriculture is widely recognized as a crucial dual-purpose strategy. It helps
mitigate climate change (primarily through carbon sequestration in soils, reducing net GHG emissions from agriculture)
and supports adaptation to climate impacts (e.g., better resilience to droughts, floods, erratic rainfall via improved soil
structure).
At the same time, it enhances long-term agricultural productivity, yield stability, and nutritional quality of food, making
it a key approach for ensuring food security in a warming world with increasing climate stresses.
Reports highlight that these practices build resilient food systems capable of sustaining production amid population
growth and environmental challenges.
Statement II is correct: Farming practices such as crop diversification (including crop rotations), cover cropping, and
reduced tillage (or no-till/conservation tillage) are core regenerative techniques. These practices improve soil
structure, soil organic matter, and microbial activity. This leads to better water retention, improved nutrient
availability, and reduced soil erosion, which enhance agricultural productivity.
These practices also increase carbon sequestration in soils, thereby reducing the concentration of atmospheric carbon
dioxide and contributing to climate change mitigation.
Statement II explains Statement I:
Because regenerative practices improve soil health and water-holding capacity, they support sustained crop productivity
and yield stability, which strengthens food security. At the same time, the sequestration of carbon and improved
ecosystem resilience help address climate change. Therefore, Statement II provides the underlying mechanism that
explains why regenerative agriculture can address both food security and climate change challenges.
Hence, Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.text: With reference to the Indian economy, the term “gilt-edged market” refers to the market for which of the following?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
In the context of the Indian economy, the term “gilt-edged market” (or gilt-edged securities market) specifically refers
to the market for government securities (also known as G-Secs). These include treasury bills, dated securities
(government bonds), and other debt instruments issued by the Central Government (and sometimes state
governments).
The name "gilt-edged" originates from the historical practice in the UK (and adopted in India and other Commonwealth
countries) where high-quality government bonds had gilded (gold-edged) borders on their certificates, symbolizing their
superior quality and safety.
Key reasons why government securities are called gilt-edged:
● They are considered virtually risk-free (zero default risk) because they are backed by the sovereign guarantee of the
Government of India.
● They offer stable, predictable returns through fixed interest (coupon) payments.
● They are highly liquid and form a major part of the debt market regulated by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).

questions.text: Consider the following scenarios: I. The government expands public investment in primary healthcare facilities II. A company establishes a vocational training institute to enhance the skills of its workforce. III. The government constructs a new expressway to improve transportation and logistics efficiency. IV. An individual spends money on higher education and professional training. V. A manufacturing firm purchases new automated machinery to increase production. How many of the above are examples of investment in human capital formation?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
Human capital formation refers to the process of enhancing the knowledge, skills, health, and productive capacities of
individuals through deliberate investments in areas such as education, healthcare & nutrition and training & skill
development.
It focuses on improving the quality of the labour force, enabling individuals to become more productive and contribute
more effectively to economic growth and national development.
Key sources of human capital formation include:
1. Education (schools, universities, professional courses)
2. Skill development and vocational training
3. Healthcare and nutrition
4. On-the-job training and research
A well-developed stock of human capital leads to higher productivity, technological innovation, better income levels,
and sustainable economic growth. Hence, investments in human capital are considered crucial for long-term
development of a country.
Option I is correct: Expansion of public healthcare facilities and nutrition programmes improves the health status,
physical capacity, and productivity of individuals. A healthier population is able to work more efficiently and for longer
periods.
Therefore, expenditure on healthcare and nutrition is considered an important component of human capital
formation.
Option II is correct: Establishing a vocational training institute enhances the technical and professional skills of
workers. Skill development improves the efficiency, employability, and productivity of the workforce.

[31]

Hence, such investments directly contribute to human capital formation.
Option III is incorrect: Construction of an expressway improves transportation networks, reduces logistics costs, and
facilitates trade and mobility. However, it represents investment in physical infrastructure, which is a form of physical
capital formation, not human capital formation.
Option IV is correct: Spending on higher education and professional training increases an individual’s knowledge,
expertise, and productive capabilities. Such educational investment improves earning potential and productivity, making
it a key example of human capital formation.
Option V is incorrect: Purchasing automated machinery increases the productive capacity of a firm by adding to its
stock of machines and equipment. This is an example of physical capital formation, as it does not directly enhance the
skills, education, or health of individuals.

questions.text: With reference to the global economy, which of the following best describes the term friendshoring, recently seen in the news?
questions.option_a: A policy of relocating production and supply chains to geographically neighbouring countries to reduce transportation costs.
questions.option_b: A policy of extending long-term credit facilities and development finance to countries regarded as close strategic partners.
questions.option_c: A policy of relocating production and supply chains to countries regarded as political and economic allies.
questions.option_d: A policy of giving preferential market access to goods and services originating from countries considered close diplomatic partners.
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
The term ‘Friendshoring’ (also called allyshoring) refers to an emerging strategy in the global economy where countries
and multinational companies shift production facilities and supply chains to nations that are trusted political and
economic partners or geopolitical allies. The objective is to reduce geopolitical risks, enhance supply-chain resilience,
and avoid over-dependence on rival or unstable countries.
● The concept gained prominence after disruptions caused by events such as the COVID-19 pandemic and rising
geopolitical tensions (such as the Russia–Ukraine War), which exposed vulnerabilities in highly concentrated global
supply chains and when several countries sought to make supply chains more resilient by relying on trusted allies
rather than purely cost-efficient locations.
● Under friendshoring, production is not merely moved for cost advantages or geographic proximity, but to countries
that share strategic, political, or economic alignment.
Knowledge Base: Some other related supply-chain strategies used in the global economy:
1. Nearshoring:
It refers to the relocation of production or business processes to nearby or neighbouring countries to reduce
transportation costs, improve supply-chain efficiency, and shorten delivery time. Geographic proximity is the key factor.
2. Offshoring:
It means shifting production or services to distant foreign countries, usually to benefit from lower labour costs,
cheaper resources, or favourable regulations.
3. Reshoring (Onshoring):
It refers to bringing manufacturing and production activities back to the home country after they were previously
moved abroad. Governments often encourage this to strengthen domestic industries and employment.
4. Diversified Supply-Chain Strategy:
Instead of relying on one country, firms spread production and sourcing across multiple countries to reduce risks
arising from geopolitical tensions, trade disputes, or disruptions.

questions.text: Consider the following statements regarding India’s Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), the Digital Rupee (e₹): I. It is a legal tender. II. The RBI has introduced two variants of the Digital Rupee; e₹-W (wholesale) for interbank and financial market transactions and e₹-R (retail) for consumer and merchant payments. III. Unlike bank deposits, the Digital Rupee kept in e₹ wallet does not earn interest. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.option_d: I, II and III dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
The Reserve Bank of India has recently proposed a unified cross-border digital payment system linking the Digital
Rupee with the CBDCs of BRICS countries.
Statement I is correct:Digital Rupee or e₹, is India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC). It is the digital form of India's
physical currency, the Rupee (₹). e₹ is issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in digital form and e₹ is at par with the
physical currency, offering features similar to physical cash like convenience of use, guarantee of RBI, finality of
settlement, etc. e₹ is stored in the user's digital wallet and can be used to receive / send money, and / or make payment
for transactions, just like any physical ₹ note.
In terms of Section 26 of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Act, 1934, every e₹ bank note shall be legal tender at any place
in India in payment or on account for the amount expressed therein and is guaranteed by the Central Government. e₹,
being a digital form of the ₹ bank note, is legal tender and is the liability of the Reserve Bank of India.
The logo and tagline for India’s CBDC is as under:
Statement II is correct: The RBI has introduced two variants of CBDC:
● e₹-W (Wholesale CBDC): Used mainly by banks and financial institutions for interbank transactions and settlement
of government securities.
● e₹-R (Retail CBDC): Designed for public use, enabling individuals and businesses to conduct everyday payments.
Statement III is correct: Given the cash-like features of e₹, there is no interest payable on the e₹ wallet balances.The
Digital Rupee does not earn interest, unlike bank deposits kept in savings or fixed deposit accounts. It functions
similarly to physical cash. However, it maintains full convertibility and can be exchanged for bank deposits or physical
currency, ensuring parity with existing forms of money.

questions.text: Consider the following transactions: I. Salaries paid to employees of the Government of India. II. Remittances sent by Indians working abroad to their relatives in India. III. An Indian citizen selling his 5 year old used car to his friend. IV. Brokerage earned by a share broker in India on the sale of equity shares. V. Purchase of a newly constructed residential house by an Indian individual. How many of the above are included in the calculation of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of India?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.

[33]

Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the total monetary value of all final goods and services produced within the domestic
territory of a country during a specific period, usually one year. It is the most widely used indicator to measure the size
and performance of an economy.
GDP includes production carried out within the geographical boundaries of the country, irrespective of whether the
producers are citizens or foreigners.
Key Features of GDP
● It measures only final goods and services to avoid double counting.
● It includes current production within the country during the given period.
● It is measured in monetary terms to aggregate diverse goods and services.
● It reflects economic activity within the domestic territory.
Major Components of GDP
GDP is commonly calculated using the expenditure method:
● GDP = C + I + G + (X − M)
Where:
● C – Private Final Consumption Expenditure
● I – Gross Investment (capital formation)
● G – Government Final Consumption Expenditure
● X − M – Net Exports (Exports − Imports)
Transactions Not Included in GDP:
Certain transactions are excluded because they do not represent current production, such as:
● Transfer payments (pensions, remittances, scholarships)
● Sale/purchage of Second-hand goods
● Pure financial transactions (purchase of shares/bonds)
● Illegal economic activities (in official statistics)
Option I is correct: Salaries paid to employees of the Government of India – Included
Government employees provide services such as administration, defence, policing, etc. The wages and salaries paid by the
government are payments for current services, therefore they form part of government final consumption expenditure
and are included in GDP.
Option II is incorrect: Remittances sent by Indians working abroad to their relatives in India – Not Included
These are transfer payments and do not arise from production within India. Since GDP counts only the value of goods
and services produced domestically, such remittances are excluded.
Option III is incorrect: A citizen selling his five year old used car to his friend – Not Included
The car was produced in an earlier year and its value was already counted in GDP when it was originally produced.
Including it again would lead to double counting. Hence, sale of second-hand goods is excluded (though any brokerage
involved would be counted).
Option IV is correct: Brokerage earned by a share broker on the sale of equity shares – Included
The shares themselves are financial assets, so their transfer is not counted. However, the brokerage represents payment
for a service provided during the current period, so it contributes to GDP.
Hence, payment for this service is therefore included in the calculation of GDP.
Option V is correct: Purchase of a newly constructed residential house by an Indian Individual– Included
A newly built house is a final good produced in the current period. It represents new production in the construction
sector and is included in GDP under gross capital formation (investment expenditure).

questions.text: Consider the following economic activities: I. Processing of raw cotton into finished textile garments in a factory. II. Construction of residential buildings by a real estate company III. Conversion of iron ore into steel in a steel plant. IV. Banking services provided to customers through commercial banks. V. Manufacturing of cement using limestone and other raw materials. How many of the above activities belong to the secondary sector of the economy?
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
Economic activities in an economy are commonly classified into three broad sectors based on the nature of the
activity.
1. The Primary Sector includes activities that involve the extraction or direct use of natural resources, such as
agriculture, fishing, forestry, mining, and animal husbandry.
2. The Secondary Sector consists of activities related to manufacturing and construction, where raw materials
obtained from the primary sector are processed or transformed into finished or semi-finished goods, such as steel,
textiles, cement, and machinery.
3. The Tertiary Sector, also known as the service sector, includes activities that provide services rather than producing
tangible goods, such as banking, transport, trade, communication, education, healthcare, and tourism.
Option I is correct: Processing of raw cotton into finished textile garments in a factory:
This involves manufacturing, where raw cotton is converted into garments. Hence, it belongs to the Secondary Sector.
Option II is correct: Construction of residential buildings by a real estate company:
Construction is considered part of the Secondary Sector because it involves transforming raw materials (cement, steel,
bricks) into infrastructure.
Option III is correct: Conversion of iron ore into steel in a steel plant:
This is a manufacturing activity, where raw material (iron ore) is processed into steel. Hence, it belongs to the
Secondary Sector.
Option IV is incorrect: Banking services provided to customers through commercial banks:
Banking provides financial services and does not involve production of goods. Therefore, it belongs to the tertiary
(service) sector.
Option V is correct: Manufacturing of cement using limestone and other raw materials:
This involves industrial production, converting raw materials into cement, so it belongs to the Secondary Sector.
Knowledge Base:

[35]

questions.text: The depreciation of the Indian Rupee against major global currencies can be addressed through several actions by the Government of India and the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). In this context, consider the following actions: I. Increase in the policy repo rate by the Reserve Bank of India II. Purchase of large quantities of US dollars by the RBI from the foreign exchange market. III. Relaxation of norms for External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs) by the RBI. IV. Reduction in customs duties on a wide range of imported goods by the Government of India. How many of the above actions are likely to prevent the depreciation of the Indian rupee?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
The value of the Indian Rupee in the foreign exchange market is determined by the demand and supply of foreign
currencies, particularly the US dollar. When the demand for foreign currency rises relative to the rupee, the rupee
tends to depreciate against major global currencies.
To manage excessive volatility or depreciation, the Reserve Bank of India and the Government of India may adopt various
monetary, fiscal, and external sector policy measures, such as influencing capital flows, regulating imports, and
intervening in the foreign exchange market. These steps aim to stabilize the currency and maintain external sector
stability.
Option I is correct: Increase in the policy repo rate by the Reserve Bank of India
An increase in the repo rate raises domestic interest rates. Higher interest rates tend to attract foreign portfolio
investment into government securities and financial markets, increasing the demand for the rupee and helping stabilize
the currency by addressing its depreciation.
Option II is incorrect: Purchase of large quantities of US dollars by the RBI from the foreign exchange market
When the RBI purchases US dollars, it increases the demand for dollars and injects rupees into the market. This can
further weaken (depreciate) the rupee rather than support (appreciate) it. To defend the rupee, the RBI typically sells
dollars, not buys them.
Option III is correct: Relaxation of norms for External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs) by the Reserve Bank of India
Relaxing ECB norms allows Indian firms to borrow more from international markets, bringing foreign currency inflows
into the country. These inflows increase the supply of foreign exchange, helping reduce pressure on the rupee.
Option IV is incorrect: Reduction in customs duties on a wide range of imported goods
Reducing import duties makes imports cheaper and can increase import demand. This raises the demand for foreign
currency, which can worsen the depreciation of the rupee.

questions.text: With reference to the proclamation of National Emergency under Article 352 of the Constitution of India, consider the following statements: I. A National Emergency can be proclaimed on the grounds of war, external aggression, or armed rebellion. II. The freedoms guaranteed under Article 19 automatically remain suspended during a National Emergency irrespective of the ground on which it is declared. III. The President can proclaim a National Emergency only after receiving a written recommendation from the Prime Minister. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
The provision for National Emergency is contained in Article 352 of the Constitution of India. It empowers the President
of India to proclaim an emergency when the security of India or any part of its territory is threatened by war, external
aggression, or armed rebellion.
Statement I is correct: Under Article 352 of the Constitution of India, the President can proclaim a National Emergency
on three grounds:

[36]

● War
● External aggression
● Armed rebellion
Originally, the Constitution used the term “internal disturbance,” but it was replaced by “armed rebellion” by the 44th
Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978 to prevent misuse of emergency powers.
The resolution approving the proclamation of National Emergency must be approved by both Houses of Parliament
within one month from the date of its issuance. The approval must be passed by a special majority (meaning a majority
of the total membership of the House and not less than two-thirds of the members present and voting) in either house of
Parliament.
● Originally, the Constitution provided that a proclamation of National Emergency had to be approved by both Houses
of Parliament within two months.
● However, the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978 reduced this approval period to one month.
● Once the proclamation is approved by both Houses of Parliament the National Emergency continues for six months. It
can be extended indefinitely, but each extension requires fresh approval by Parliament every six months.
Statement II is incorrect: The suspension of freedoms guaranteed under Article 19 during a National Emergency is
governed by Article 358. According to this provision, Article 19 is automatically suspended only when the Emergency is
proclaimed on the grounds of war or external aggression. However, if the Emergency is declared on the ground of
armed rebellion, Article 19 does not get automatically suspended, but rather continues to operate.
Statement III is incorrect: The President can proclaim a National Emergency only on the written recommendation of
the Union Cabinet, not merely the Prime Minister.
This safeguard was introduced by the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978 to ensure that the decision is taken
collectively by the Cabinet rather than by a single individual.

questions.text: Consider the following pairs: Subject Explicitly mentioned under (List of Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India) I. Fisheries State List II. Insurance Union List III. Education State List IV. Cyber Security Concurrent List How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.option_d: All the four dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
The Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India defines the distribution of legislative powers between the Union and
the States, forming the basis of India’s federal structure. It contains three lists of subjects on which different levels of
government can make laws.
● Union List (List I): Subjects of national importance on which only Parliament can legislate (e.g., defence, foreign
affairs, currency, insurance).
● State List (List II): Subjects primarily of local or state importance on which State Legislatures normally legislate
(e.g., public order, police, public health, fisheries).
● Concurrent List (List III): Subjects on which both Parliament and State Legislatures can make laws (e.g., education,
forests, marriage and divorce). In case of conflict, the Union law prevails.
Additionally, residuary powers (subjects not mentioned in any of the three lists) are vested in Parliament under Article 248
and Entry 97 of the Union List.
Pair I is correct: Fisheries
Fisheries fall under the State List (Entry 21), which includes fisheries within territorial waters and inland fisheries.
Therefore, the State Legislatures have the power to make laws regarding fisheries within their territorial waters.
Pair II is correct: Insurance

[37]

“Insurance” is included in the Union List (Entry 47). Hence, only Parliament has the power to legislate on matters
relating to insurance.
Pair III is incorrect: Education
Originally, education was a subject in the State List. However, it was shifted to the Concurrent List by the 42nd
Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976. Now both Parliament and State Legislatures can make laws on education.
Pair IV is incorrect: Cyber security
“Cyber security” is not explicitly mentioned in any of the three lists of the Seventh Schedule. Therefore, it falls under
residuary powers, which belong to Parliament under Article 248 of the Constitution of India and Entry 97 of the Union
List. Hence, it is not part of the Concurrent List.

questions.text: Consider the following pairs: Regions often Reason for being in mentioned in news news I. Rakhine State Conflict involving the Rohingya population and armed ethnic groups in Myanmar II. Durand Line Disputed border between Pakistan and Afghanistan leading to recurring tensions III. Golan Heights Territorial dispute between Israel and Lebanon over strategic border areas How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
Pair I is correct: Rakhine State
Rakhine State is located in western Myanmar, bordering Bangladesh.
● The region has been in the news mainly due to the long-standing crisis involving the Rohingya, a Muslim minority
group in Myanmar.
● Violence and military operations by the Myanmar armed forces, along with clashes involving local insurgent ethnic
groups such as the Arakan Army, have led to massive displacement of people.
● A large number of Rohingya refugees fled to Bangladesh, making the issue a major humanitarian and geopolitical
concern in South Asia.
Pair II is correct: Durand Line
The Durand Line is the 2,640-km boundary separating Pakistan and Afghanistan, demarcated in 1893 during British
colonial rule.
● Pakistan recognizes it as the international border, but Afghanistan has historically contested its legitimacy.
● Because of this disagreement, along with issues related to militant movement and border fencing, the Durand Line
frequently becomes a source of diplomatic tensions and occasional clashes between the two countries.
Pair III is incorrect: Golan Heights
The Golan Heights is a strategic plateau originally part of Syria.
● It was captured by Israel during the Six-Day War (1967) and later annexed by Israel in 1981, though this annexation is
not widely recognized internationally.
● The dispute over the Golan Heights is therefore primarily between Israel and Syria, not between Israel and
Lebanon.
● Lebanon’s territorial disputes with Israel mainly involve areas such as the Blue line and Shebaa Farms, not the Golan
Heights.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: The European Union has recently enforced the Artificial Intelligence Act (EU AI Act), establishing the world’s first comprehensive legal framework for the regulation of Artificial Intelligence. Statement II: To ensure absolute user privacy and prevent online child abuse, the EU AI Act imposes a complete ban on all ‘Agentic AI’ systems and ‘General Purpose AI (GPAI) models’ within its member states. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: The European Union Artificial Intelligence Act (EU AI Act) passed by the European Union is
regarded as the world’s first comprehensive legal framework for regulating Artificial Intelligence. It aims to ensure that
AI systems used in the EU are safe, transparent, and respect fundamental rights while promoting innovation.
The Act applies to AI developers, providers, and users whose systems are placed on the EU market or used within EU
member states, even if the company is located outside the EU.
A key feature of the Act is its risk-based regulatory approach, under which AI systems are classified into four
categories:
● Unacceptable Risk: AI practices that are completely prohibited (e.g., social scoring by governments,
manipulative AI systems that exploit vulnerable groups).
● High Risk: AI systems used in sensitive sectors such as healthcare, education, employment, law enforcement,
and critical infrastructure. These are permitted but subject to strict requirements like risk assessment, human
oversight, and transparency.
● Limited Risk: AI systems such as chatbots that must disclose that users are interacting with AI.
● Minimal Risk: Applications such as spam filters or AI in video games that face minimal regulatory obligations.
The Act also prescribes strict penalties for violations, which can reach up to €35 million or 7% of a company’s global
annual turnover, whichever is higher.
Statement II is incorrect: The EU AI Act does not impose a complete ban on “Agentic AI” systems or General Purpose
AI (GPAI) models.
● Instead, the Act regulates AI systems depending on the level of risk they pose. General-purpose AI models;
such as large language models used in generative AI are allowed but subject to certain obligations, including
transparency requirements, technical documentation, and safeguards against misuse.
● Only specific harmful AI practices classified as “unacceptable risk” are prohibited under the Act. These include
manipulative AI systems, exploitative technologies targeting vulnerable groups, and government social-scoring
systems.
Knowledge Base: Uses and Objectives of the EU AI Act
The Act seeks to establish a balanced framework for the development and use of Artificial Intelligence in Europe. Its
major objectives include:
● Protecting fundamental rights such as privacy, non-discrimination, and human dignity.
● Ensuring safety and reliability of AI systems used in critical sectors.
● Promoting transparency and accountability in algorithmic decision-making.
● Creating a harmonised regulatory environment across EU member states.
By establishing clear legal rules, the EU also aims to encourage responsible AI innovation while maintaining public trust
in digital technologies.

questions.text: Arrange the following languages in the chronological order with respect to the year in which they were granted the status of “Classical Language” by the Government of India: I. Odia II. Tamil III. Sanskrit IV. Prakrit Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
The Government of India introduced the category of Classical Languages in 2004 to recognize languages that possess
high antiquity, a rich and ancient literary tradition, and an independent cultural heritage. As of 2025, a total of 11 Indian
languages hold the status of Classical Language.
Among the languages given in the question, the chronological order in which they were granted Classical Language
status is as follows:
1. Tamil (Option II) – Tamil was the first language to be granted Classical Language status in 2004. It has a continuous
literary tradition dating back more than two millennia, including the Sangam literature.
2. Sanskrit (Option III) – Sanskrit was granted Classical Language status in 2005. It is one of the world’s oldest recorded
languages and forms the basis of a vast corpus of ancient Indian literature, including the Vedas, Upanishads, epics, and
classical philosophical texts.
3. Odia (Option I) – Odia received Classical Language status in 2014. The language has a long literary history with works
such as Sarala Das’s Odia Mahabharata and early inscriptions demonstrating its antiquity.
4. Prakrit (Option IV) – Prakrit was granted Classical Language status in 2024. The Prakrit languages were widely used
in ancient India and appear in Jain and Buddhist literature as well as inscriptions such as those of Emperor Ashoka.
Thus, the correct chronological order in which the given languages received the status of Classical Language is: Tamil
→ Sanskrit → Odia → Prakrit
Hence, the correct answer is d) II – III – I – IV.
Knowledge Base: Criteria for a language to be classified as a Classical Language in India:
The Government of India, through the Ministry of Culture and in consultation with linguistic and historical experts, has
established criteria for designating a language as a Classical Language.
The criteria for a language to be classified as a Classical Language include the following:
● High antiquity of its early texts or recorded history, spanning a period of 1,500-2,000 years.
● A body of ancient literature or texts that is considered heritage by generations of speakers.
● Knowledge texts, especially prose texts, in addition to poetry, epigraphical and inscriptional evidence.
● The Classical Language and its literature may be distinct from its current form or show discontinuity with later
forms derived from the original.

questions.text: With reference to the ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 (ODI), consider the following statements: I. The tournament was primarily hosted by India. II. India won its first-ever ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup title in this tournament. III. India defeated Australia in the final by 58 runs to win the championship. IV. The tournament featured a total of 12 teams. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
The ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 attracted considerable attention as it marked an important milestone for
Indian women’s cricket and was widely discussed in the sporting landscape. It was played from 30 September to 2
November 2025.
Statement I is correct. The ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 was hosted by India. The tournament was organised
under the authority of the International Cricket Council and marked the return of the women’s ODI World Cup to India
after a significant gap. Hosting such a prestigious global event reflects the growing infrastructure, popularity and
administrative capability of Indian cricket. Several major venues across the country were used for the matches,
highlighting India’s increasing role in promoting women’s international cricket.
Sri Lanka was chosen as a neutral venue for some specific matches involving Pakistan.
Statement II is correct. India achieved a historic milestone by winning its first-ever ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup
title in 2025. Prior to this victory, the Indian women’s team had reached the final on a few occasions but had not been
able to secure the championship.
Statement III is incorrect. India won the ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025 final against South Africa, not Australia.
The match was played at Dr. D. Y. Patil Stadium, where India secured a 52-run victory to clinch the title.
Statement IV is incorrect. The ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2025, featuring eight teams (not 12) in a round-robin
format with 28 league matches, was played from 30 September to 2 November 2025.

questions.text: Consider the following pairs: Area of conflict Country where it mentioned in news is located I. Natanz Turkey II. Darfur Sudan III. Novorossiysk Ukraine How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Pair I is incorrect. Natanz Nuclear Facility is located in Iran, not in Turkey. Natanz is one of the most important sites
associated with Iran’s nuclear enrichment programme and has repeatedly been in the global spotlight due to tensions
between Iran and Western powers regarding nuclear development. The facility lies in central Iran, in Isfahan Province,
and has been linked to several cyber-attacks and sabotage incidents over the years.

[41]

Pair II is correct. Darfur is a large region located in western Sudan. It has been the site of a prolonged humanitarian and
ethnic conflict since the early 2000s, often referred to as the Darfur crisis. The conflict emerged due to tensions between
government-backed militias and rebel groups representing non-Arab communities. The region continues to be mentioned
in global news due to ongoing instability, displacement of civilians, and international humanitarian concerns.
Pair III is incorrect. Novorossiysk is actually located in Russia, not Ukraine. It is a major Russian port city on the Black
Sea and hosts an important naval base of the Russian Black Sea Fleet. Recently, Russia declared a state of emergency at
the port after Ukrainian drone attack had damaged residential buildings and injured at least five people, including two
children.

questions.text: Which among the following countries has recently become the 11th full member of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN)?
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Timor-Leste, also known as East Timor, has recently been admitted as the 11th full member of the Association of
Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) during the 47th ASEAN Summit in Malaysia’s Kuala Lumpur in October 2025. . This
marks the first expansion of ASEAN since the 1990s, when countries such as Vietnam, Laos, Myanmar and Cambodia
joined the grouping.
ASEAN is a regional organisation of Southeast Asian countries formed in 1967 to promote economic growth, political
cooperation, and regional stability. Membership in ASEAN allows countries to participate in regional economic
integration mechanisms, including free trade arrangements, investment flows, and wider market access within the
region.
By becoming a full member, Timor-Leste gains access to ASEAN’s trade frameworks, regional supply chains, and
development cooperation programmes. This is expected to support the country’s economic development, infrastructure
growth, and deeper integration with Southeast Asian economies.

questions.text: Consider the following substances: I. Potassium Nitrate as an oxidising agent II. Barium compounds to produce green colour III. Sulphur as a reducing agent How many of the above substances are generally used in high quantities as primary components in manufacturing of ‘green firecrackers’?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Firecrackers have long been associated with festivals and celebrations in India. However, conventional firecrackers
release large amounts of particulate matter and toxic gases into the atmosphere. These emissions worsen air pollution,
especially during winter months when atmospheric conditions already favour pollution accumulation.
To address this problem, scientists in India developed Green Firecrackers. These crackers are designed to reduce
harmful emissions by about 30% compared to traditional firecrackers. They achieve this by modifying the chemical
composition of fireworks. Certain toxic substances used in conventional firecrackers are either removed or reduced,
while safer alternatives are introduced.
Option I is correct. Green firecrackers use Potassium Nitrate as an oxidising agent. Oxidising agents supply oxygen
required for the combustion reaction. In green firecrackers, potassium nitrate is preferred because it helps control the
intensity of the reaction while reducing the emission of particulate matter.
On the other hand, Nitrates, Chlorates, and perchlorates are incorporated in traditional firecrackers as the oxidizing
agents.
Also, the Aluminium powder is commonly used in fireworks to produce bright flashes and sparkling effects. However,
aluminium particles contribute to fine particulate matter (PM) in the atmosphere. In green firecrackers, aluminium is not
completely removed, but its quantity is significantly reduced. This controlled use ensures that the visual effect of
fireworks is retained while limiting pollution levels.
Option II is incorrect. In conventional firecrackers, copper and barium salts are widely used to produce the green
colour/green flames in fireworks. However, barium compounds are highly toxic and contribute to environmental
pollution. Green firecrackers are specifically designed without the use of copper or barium compounds. Their removal is

[43]

one of the key changes that make green crackers less harmful to both human health and the environment compared to
traditional firecrackers.
Option III is incorrect. In traditional firecrackers, sulphur acts as a reducing agent and helps sustain the combustion
reaction. It is usually combined with charcoal and oxidising agents to produce the explosive effect. However, sulphur-
based formulations lead to release of sulphur dioxide and other pollutants. Green firecrackers therefore avoid such
traditional reducing agents, replacing them with safer chemical combinations to reduce pollution.

questions.text: With reference to the United Nations (UN) Peacekeeping Operations, consider the following statements: I. They are deployed under UN mandates to protect civilians and support peace in conflict-affected regions. II. The United States of America is currently the largest financial contributor of the UN peacekeeping budget. III. India has traditionally been among the largest troop-contributing countries to UN peacekeeping missions. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
The United Nations Peacekeeping Operations represent one of the most practical tools available to the international
community for maintaining peace in conflict-prone regions. These missions are usually deployed in places where fragile
ceasefires exist or where societies are emerging from violent conflict.
Statement I is correct. United Nations peacekeeping missions are established through mandates authorised by the UN
Security Council. These mandates define the objectives of each mission. A central responsibility of peacekeepers today is
the protection of civilians, particularly in areas affected by armed conflict. In addition, peacekeepers help monitor
ceasefires, reduce violence, assist in implementing peace agreements and support the rebuilding of institutions that
promote long-term stability.
Statement II is correct. Peacekeeping operations are financed through assessed contributions from UN member states.
The share that each country contributes is determined through a formula approved by the UN General Assembly. Among
all contributors, the United States is the largest financial contributor, while China is the second largest contributor.
Their combined financial support constitutes a substantial share of the overall peacekeeping budget, approaching nearly
half of the total contributions.
Statement III is correct. India has maintained a long-standing and respected presence in UN peacekeeping missions
since the early years of the organisation. Thousands of Indian soldiers, police personnel and military observers have
served in missions across Africa, Asia and the Middle East. Since the 1950s, India has sent over 290,000 peacekeepers to
more than 50 missions worldwide, making it the largest contributor to UN peacekeeping efforts. Today, over 5,000
Indian troops serve in nine of the eleven active missions, often in dangerous and hostile regions, dedicated to upholding
global peace and security.
Indian peacekeepers are widely known for their professional conduct, operational capability and humanitarian
engagement with local communities. Because of this sustained participation, India continues to be recognised as one of
the major troop-contributing countries to UN peacekeeping operations

questions.text: With reference to modern Indian history, M. Veeraraghavachariar, G. Subramania Iyer, and P. Ananda Charlu are associated with the establishment of which of the following organisations?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
In the decades preceding the formation of the Indian National Congress, several regional political associations emerged
in different presidencies of British India. These organisations played a crucial role in articulating public grievances,
promoting political awareness and preparing the ground for organised nationalist politics.
They were often led by educated Indian elites such as lawyers, journalists and social reformers, who sought greater
representation of Indians in administration and policy-making. Many of these bodies later became important feeders to
the early nationalist movement.
The Madras Mahajana Sabha was established in 1884 in Madras. It was founded by M. Veeraraghavachariar, G.
Subramania Iyer, and P. Ananda Charlu. The organisation aimed to promote political awareness, discuss public issues
and represent Indian grievances before the colonial government. It soon became one of the most influential political
bodies in South India and later played an important role in the early sessions of the Indian National Congress.
Knowledge Base:
● The Madras Native Association was one of the earliest political organisations in the Madras Presidency. It was
founded in 1852 by Gazulu Lakshminarasu Chetty. Its objective was to represent the interests of Indians before the
British authorities and to raise issues concerning governance and administration.
● The Justice Party was founded later in 1916 in Madras. It was led by Dr. T. M. Nair, P. Tyagaraja Chetty, and C. N.
Mudaliar. The party represented the interests of non-Brahmin and intermediate castes and opposed the perceived
dominance of Brahmins in administration, education and politics. It was also linked with the formation of the South
Indian Liberal Federation (SILF).
● The Bombay Presidency Association was founded in 1885 by Pherozeshah Mehta, Badruddin Tyabji, and K. T.
Telang. It functioned as an important political organisation in the Bombay Presidency and worked towards
constitutional reforms and increased Indian participation in governance.

questions.text: With reference to Ancient Indian inscriptions, consider the following pairs: Inscription Issued by/Associated Ruler I. Naneghat Gautmiputra Satakarni Inscription (Satavahana Dynasty) II. Aihole Pulakeshin II (Chalukya Inscription Dynasty) III. Uttarmerur Rajaraja I (Chola Inscription Dynasty) How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Inscriptions constitute one of the most reliable primary sources for reconstructing ancient Indian history. Unlike
literary texts, which may contain exaggerations or later interpolations, inscriptions are contemporary records engraved
on stone, pillars or copper plates. They provide valuable information about rulers, dynasties, administrative practices,
religious patronage and social conditions of their time.
Pair I is incorrect. The Naneghat inscription, located in present-day Maharashtra, is associated with the Satavahana
dynasty. It was issued by Queen Naganika in Ist Century BCE (not by Gautamiputra Satakarni 106 to 130 CE), the wife of
Satakarni I, one of the prominent early Satavahana rulers. Queen Naganika refers to him with the title
“Dakshinapathapati” (Lord of the Deccan). This title indicates that Satakarni I had established considerable authority over
the Dakshinapatha or the Deccan region.
The inscription also records royal genealogies and Vedic sacrifices performed by the Satavahana rulers, such as the
Ashvamedha. It is an important source for understanding the early political history of the Satavahanas in the Deccan
region.

[45]

Pair II is correct. The Aihole inscription, also known as the Aihole Prashasti, dates to around 634 CE. It was composed
by the court poet Ravikirti during the reign of Pulakeshin II of the Chalukya dynasty. The inscription glorifies Pulakeshin
II’s achievements and notably records his victory over Harshavardhana on the banks of the Narmada.
Pair III is incorrect. The Uttarmerur inscriptions, found in Tamil Nadu, belong to the Chola period, but they are mainly
associated with the reign of Parantaka I (10th century CE) rather than Rajaraja I. These inscriptions are historically
significant because they provide detailed information about the functioning of local self-government in Chola villages,
particularly the Kudavolai system of electing members to village assemblies.

questions.text: With reference to the Vernacular Press Act, 1878 in modern Indian history, consider the following statements: Statement I: It was enacted during the viceroyalty of Lord Lytton. Statement II: Vernacular newspapers had begun to criticise British policies like their famine policy and influence public opinion against colonial rule. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I.
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct. dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
During the nineteenth century, newspapers gradually emerged as an important instrument of political discussion and
public criticism of colonial policies. While English newspapers were largely read by Europeans and a small educated elite,
vernacular newspapers reached a much wider Indian readership. As a result, the British administration increasingly
perceived the Indian-language press as a potential threat to colonial authority. It was in this political climate that the
colonial government introduced the Vernacular Press Act of 1878, one of the most controversial press laws in British
India.
Statement I is correct. The Vernacular Press Act, 1878 was enacted during the viceroyalty of Lord Lytton. The Act
empowered colonial authorities, particularly District Magistrates, to take action against newspapers published in Indian
vernacular languages if their writings were considered to be seditious or hostile to the government. Notably, the law
applied only to vernacular newspapers, while English-language newspapers were not subjected to the same restrictions.
Statement II is correct. By the 1870s, several vernacular newspapers had begun to criticise British administrative
policies and raise political awareness among Indians. The colonial government feared that such writings could influence
public opinion and encourage opposition to British rule.
Statement II explains Statement I. The growing criticism of colonial policies in vernacular newspapers was precisely the
reason why the British government enacted the Vernacular Press Act, 1878 to curb dissent expressed through the Indian-
language press. During this period, vernacular newspapers (like Amrita Bazar Patrika and Soma Prakash) were highly
critical of the British government’s handling of the Great Famine of 1876–78 and the expensive Second Anglo-Afghan War.
Also, in 1877 the Delhi Durbar , was organised by Lytton to proclaim Queen Victoria as Empress of India, as a lavish, 17-day
event held amid the devastating Great Famine of 1876–1878. The vernacular newspapers were critical of such policies of
the British.

questions.text: With reference to the Santhal Uprising (1855–56) during India’s freedom struggle, consider the following statements: I. The uprising was triggered by the exploitation of locals by zamindars, moneylenders and colonial officials in Santhal region. II. The rebellion was led by the brothers Sidhu and Kanu, who mobilised the Santhals to resist oppression and establish their own rule. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
The Santhal Uprising of 1855–56, also known as the Santhal Hul, was one of the most significant tribal revolts against
colonial rule in nineteenth-century India. It occurred in the region known as Daman-i-koh, located in present-day
Jharkhand and adjoining areas of Bihar and West Bengal.
Statement I is correct. The Santhal uprising was largely a reaction against economic exploitation and administrative
oppression. The Santhals had originally cleared forest land and began cultivation in this region. Over time, however, the
entry of zamindars, moneylenders, traders and government officials into the area deeply disrupted their traditional way
of life. These outsiders, referred to by the Santhals as “dikus”, imposed heavy rents, manipulated market practices and
charged extremely high interest on loans. Such exploitative practices gradually created widespread resentment among
the Santhal community. When peaceful protests failed and repression by local authorities intensified, the Santhals
resorted to armed rebellion
Statement II is correct. The rebellion found strong leadership in two brothers, Sidhu and Kanu, who emerged as
charismatic figures among the Santhals. Chand-Bhairav, and Phulo-Jhano—along with countless other brave tribals were
associated with the Santhal Hul. They mobilised thousands of tribal followers and called upon them to resist the
oppression of zamindars, moneylenders and colonial authorities. The rebels even envisioned restoring control over their
land and establishing their own system of governance.
Although the Santhals displayed remarkable courage and mobilised large numbers of participants, the rebellion did not
succeed in overthrowing British authority. The colonial government eventually suppressed the uprising through
superior military power and modern weapons. Despite its defeat, the rebellion remains historically important as a
powerful expression of tribal resistance against colonial exploitation. While the uprising was crushed, it forced the
British to create the separate Santhal Parganas district and enact the Santhal Parganas Tenancy Act of 1876 to protect
tribal lands.

questions.text: With reference to the Cabinet Mission Plan of 1946, consider the following statements: I. It recommended a Constituent Assembly in which certain members to be elected indirectly by the members of the provincial legislatures. II. The Union government proposed under the plan was to have control only over subjects such as defence, foreign affairs and communications. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
By the mid-1940s, the demand for Indian independence had become irresistible and the British government realised that a
constitutional settlement had to be negotiated with Indian political leaders. In this context, the Cabinet Mission was sent
to India in 1946 by the British government. The Mission consisted of Lord Pethick-Lawrence, Sir Stafford Cripps and
A.V. Alexander, and its objective was to devise a constitutional framework acceptable to the major political parties in
India and facilitate the transfer of power.
The Cabinet Mission proposed a complex constitutional scheme intended to balance the demand for national unity with
the Muslim League’s fear of domination by a Hindu-majority centre. Its recommendations included the formation of a
Constituent Assembly and a federal structure with limited powers for the Union.
Statement I is correct. The Cabinet Mission recommended the formation of a Constituent Assembly to draft the
Constitution of India. Since there was not enough time to conduct elections on the basis of universal adult franchise,
the Mission proposed that certain members of the Constituent Assembly should be elected indirectly by the already
elected provincial legislative assemblies. This arrangement allowed representation of major political groups without
organising fresh nationwide elections.

[47]

Statement II is correct. The Cabinet Mission Plan proposed a federal structure in which the Union government would
have limited powers. The central authority was to control only a few subjects of national importance, namely defence,
foreign affairs and communications, along with the power to raise finances necessary for administering these subjects.
Most other powers were to remain with the provinces and princely states, reflecting the Mission’s attempt to reassure
communities that feared an overly powerful central government.
The Cabinet Mission also introduced a grouping scheme, under which provinces were placed into three sections (A, B and
C). Provinces within each section were expected to frame a group constitution and share certain subjects in common. A
province could reconsider its membership only after the first general elections held under the new constitutional
arrangements.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: The United States is currently the world's largest exporter of crude oil by value. Statement II: The United States is currently the world's largest producer of crude oil. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I.
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct.
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Statement I is incorrect: While the United States is the top producer, it is not the world's largest exporter of crude oil
by value. That title is held by Saudi Arabia. The U.S. is also the world's leading consumer (18.7% of global demand),
meaning a significant portion of its production is used domestically.
Statement II is correct: According to the Energy Institute’s (EI) 2025 Statistical Review of World Energy, the United
States remains the world’s largest oil producer, pumping 20,135 thousand barrels daily. This output accounts for 20.8%
of global supply, nearly equaling the combined production of Saudi Arabia and Russia, which rank second and third
respectively.
Hence, Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct.

questions.text: With reference to the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) in India, consider the following statements: I. It is an initiative of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). II. It aims to shift digital commerce from a platform-centric model to an interoperable network, enabling buyers and sellers across different applications to transact with each other. III. It is based on open-sourced methodology, using open specifications and open network protocols independent of any specific platform. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
The Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) was launched in 2022 to transform and democratize the digital
commerce ecosystem in the country. It aims at promoting open networks for all aspects of exchange of goods and
services over digital or electronic networks
Statement I is incorrect: The Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) is an initiative of the Department for
Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, not the Ministry of
Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
● It was launched to democratize digital commerce by enabling wider participation of sellers and service providers.
● It envisions creating a level playing field for sellers, buyers, and service providers across India, particularly
small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).

[48]

Statement II is correct: ONDC seeks to shift digital commerce from a platform-centric model to an open, interoperable
network. This allows buyers and sellers to transact across different applications or platforms, meaning a buyer on one
application can purchase from a seller listed on another participating application within the same network.
Statement III is correct: ONDC is built on open-source methodology. It uses open specifications and open network
protocols (specifically the Beckn Protocol). These protocols are independent of any specific platform, similar to how
HTTP allows different browsers to access the web or UPI allows different bank apps to transfer money. This ensures that
no single entity controls the entire ecosystem.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: According to the International Monetary Fund (IMF), global public debt is projected to exceed 100% of global GDP by 2029, reaching the highest level since the aftermath of the Second World War. Statement II: Persistent high fiscal deficits, rising government borrowing, and growing interest obligations in major economies are key factors contributing to the surge in global public debt. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: The International Monetary Fund (IMF), in its Fiscal Monitor, has projected that global public
debt may exceed 100% of global GDP by 2029, reaching the highest level since the period following the Second World
War, reflecting a sustained rise in sovereign borrowing across many economies.
Statement II is correct and explains Statement I: Rising fiscal deficits, government borrowing, and increasing interest
payments lead to a continuous accumulation of public debt. When governments consistently spend more than their
revenues, they finance the gap through borrowing, which increases the overall debt stock and pushes the global debt-to-
GDP ratio upward over time. This structural trend in many major economies is one of the key reasons behind the IMF’s
projection of global debt exceeding 100% of GDP by 2029.
Therefore, Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.text: With reference to an economy, suppose the Private Final Consumption Expenditure (C) is ₹ 50,000 crores, Gross Domestic Capital Formation (I) is ₹ 15,000 crores, and Government Final Consumption Expenditure (G) is ₹ 12,000 crores. The economy has a Real GDP of ₹ 60,000 crores and a GDP Deflator of 125. The total Exports (X) are ₹ 8,000 crores. Which of the following statements are correct? I. Nominal GDP is ₹ 75,000 crores. II. Net Exports (X−M) is ₹ –2,000 crores. III. Total Imports (M) is ₹ 10,000 crores. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: Nominal GDP is calculated using the GDP Deflator relationship.
Nominal GDP = (Real GDP × GDP Deflator) / 100
Nominal GDP = (60,000 × 125) / 100
Nominal GDP = 75,000 crores
Statement II is correct: Using the expenditure method:
Nominal GDP = C + I + G + Net Exports (X-M) [X= Exports, M=Imports]
75,000 = 50,000 + 15,000 + 12,000 + Net Exports

[49]

75,000 = 77,000 + Net Exports
Net Exports = 75,000 − 77,000 = −2,000 crores
Statement III is correct: Net Exports is defined as:
Net Exports = Exports − Imports (X − M)
Given Exports (X) = 8,000 crores and Net Exports = −2,000 crores
−2,000 = 8,000 − M
M = 8,000 + 2,000 = 10,000 crores

questions.text: With reference to the insurance sector in India, consider the following statements: I. In India, the Insurance penetration is below the global average. II. In the FY 2024, India became the third largest insurance market globally in terms of nominal premium volumes. III. Foreign Direct Investment in the insurance sector is currently permitted up to 74% under the automatic route. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
