The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) has proposed mandatory adoption of Indian
Accounting Standards (Ind AS) for all insurers starting from April 1, 2026 to improve transparency and align reporting
with global practices.
Statement I is correct: Insurance penetration is defined as the ratio of total insurance premiums to GDP. In India,
insurance penetration is about 3.7%, which is significantly lower than the global average of about 7.3%, indicating
relatively low insurance coverage in the country.
Statement II is incorrect: India is not the third-largest insurance market globally. According to global insurance market
assessments, India ranks at the 10th position in terms of total premium volume, with around 1.8% share of global
premiums.
Statement III is incorrect: FDI in the insurance sector was earlier capped at 74%, but recent reforms have liberalized the
sector, allowing up to 100% FDI under the automatic route.

questions.text: Consider the following countries: I. Azerbaijan II. Armenia III. Turkmenistan IV. Pakistan V. Afghanistan VI. Turkey How many of the above-mentioned countries share a land border with Iran?
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Iran occupies a strategically significant position in West Asia, acting as a bridge between the Middle East, Central Asia,
and South Asia. These borders influence Iran’s security dynamics, regional diplomacy, trade routes, and ethnic linkages.
Option I is correct. Iran shares a north-western land border with Azerbaijan. The two countries are connected through
the Caucasus region, and the boundary runs along the Aras River in several stretches.
Option II is correct. Iran shares a short but strategically important border with Armenia in the north-west. This
corridor provides Iran with direct access to the South Caucasus and Eurasian trade routes.

[50]

Option III is correct. Iran shares to the north-east, a long land border with Turkmenistan, linking it with Central Asia.
This border region is important for energy cooperation and trade connectivity.
Option IV is correct. Iran shares a south-eastern land border with Pakistan, particularly with Pakistan’s Balochistan
province. The region is sensitive due to cross-border militancy and security concerns.
Option V is correct. Iran also shares a long eastern border with Afghanistan, which is strategically important due to
issues related to migration, security, and water sharing.
Option VI is correct. Iran shares, to the north-west, a border with Turkey, connecting the Middle East with Europe and
the Caucasus region. The border has historically been significant for trade routes and regional geopolitics.
Knowledge Base:

questions.text: The planet Earth initially was a barren, rocky and hot object with a thin atmosphere primarily composed of-
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
The Earth formed about 4.6 billion years ago as part of the early solar system. In its earliest stage, the planet looked very
different from what we see today. It was a hot, barren and rocky body, and the atmosphere was extremely primitive.
Unlike the present atmosphere rich in oxygen and nitrogen, the early Earth was surrounded by light gases that were
captured during planetary formation. Over millions of years, geological and atmospheric processes gradually
transformed Earth into a planet capable of supporting life.
In the earliest phase of its existence, Earth had a very thin atmosphere composed mainly of hydrogen and helium, the
lightest and most abundant gases in the early solar nebula. However, because of high temperatures and weak

[51]

gravitational retention, much of this primitive atmosphere eventually escaped into space, leading to the formation of a
secondary atmosphere through volcanic outgassing.
Option a is incorrect. The present atmosphere of Earth is dominated by nitrogen (about 78%) and oxygen (about 21%),
but these gases were not present in significant amounts during the earliest stage of Earth’s formation. Oxygen in
particular appeared much later through photosynthesis by early microorganisms.
Option c is incorrect. Carbon dioxide and nitrogen became dominant components of the secondary atmosphere, which
developed later due to volcanic eruptions releasing gases from Earth’s interior.
Option d is incorrect. Although methane and other gases may have been present in trace amounts during later
atmospheric evolution, argon and methane were not the principal gases in the earliest atmosphere of Earth.

questions.text: With reference to monsoon in India, consider the following statements: Statement I: The Tamil Nadu coast of India generally remains dry during the South-West Monsoon season. Statement II: The Tamil Nadu coast receives a major part of its annual rainfall during the Northeast Monsoon. Statement III: The Tamil Nadu coast lies parallel to the Bay of Bengal branch and falls in the rain-shadow region of the Arabian Sea branch of the South-West Monsoon. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I.
questions.option_b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct, but only one of them explains Statement I.
questions.option_c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I.
questions.option_d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct.
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
India’s monsoon system is not uniform across the country. The distribution of rainfall depends greatly on the direction
of monsoon winds, local relief, and the orientation of the coastline. A striking example of this variation is seen along the
Tamil Nadu coast, which behaves differently from most other parts of India during the monsoon season.
Statement I is correct. The Tamil Nadu coast receives comparatively little rainfall during the South-West Monsoon
months (June–September) compared to the rest of India. By the time the moisture-laden winds cross the Western Ghats
and move further eastwards, a large part of their moisture has already been released, leaving the Coromandel Coast
relatively dry compared to the western coast of India.
Statement II is correct. The coastal districts of Tamil Nadu receive a significant proportion of their annual rainfall
during October to December, when the North-East Monsoon or retreating monsoon winds move across the Bay of
Bengal and gather moisture before reaching the Coromandel Coast. This makes the early winter season the principal
rainy period for Tamil Nadu.
Statement III is correct. The orientation of the Tamil Nadu coastline and the presence of the Western Ghats play a
decisive role in limiting rainfall during the South-West Monsoon. The coastline runs almost parallel to the Bay of Bengal
branch of the monsoon, reducing the chances of strong uplift and rainfall. At the same time, the region lies on the
leeward side of the Western Ghats with respect to the Arabian Sea branch, creating a rain-shadow effect.
Statement II does not explain Statement I. The rainfall received during the North-East Monsoon simply indicates when
Tamil Nadu gets most of its rainfall, but it does not explain the cause of the dryness during the South-West Monsoon
months.
Statement III explains statement I: The Arabian Sea branch of the monsoon sheds most of its moisture on the
windward slopes of the Western Ghats, leaving the areas to the east, including Tamil Nadu, in a rain-shadow region. At
the same time, the Bay of Bengal branch of the South-West Monsoon flows almost parallel to the Tamil Nadu coast,
which limits the upward movement of moist air required for rainfall.
Therefore, the limited rainfall during the South-West Monsoon is mainly due to the rain-shadow effect and the
parallel alignment of the coastline with the monsoon winds.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Earth's lithospheric plates move horizontally over the asthenosphere as rigid units. Statement II: As we move from crust to the core, the average density of the material decreases. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I.
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct. dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
The structure of the Earth’s interior plays a crucial role in shaping many geological processes observed on the planet’s
surface. The interaction between the rigid outer layers and the relatively weaker zones beneath them helps explain the
movement of large crustal segments and the dynamic nature of the Earth.
Statement I is correct. A tectonic plate (also called lithospheric plate) is a massive, irregularly-shaped slab of solid rock,
generally composed of both continental and oceanic lithosphere. Plates move horizontally over the asthenosphere as
rigid units. The lithosphere includes the crust and top mantle with its thickness range varying between 5 and 100 km in
oceanic parts and about 200 km in the continental areas. A plate may be referred to as the continental plate or oceanic
plate depending on which of the two occupy a larger portion of the plate. The interactions at plate boundaries are
responsible for major geological activities such as earthquakes, volcanic eruptions and crustal deformation.
Statement II is incorrect: The earth was mostly in a volatile state during its primordial stage. Due to gradual increase in
density the temperature inside has increased. As a result the material inside started getting separated depending on their
densities. This allowed heavier materials (like iron) to sink towards the centre of the earth and the lighter ones to move
towards the surface. It is through the process of differentiation that the earth forming material got separated into
different layers. Starting from the surface to the central parts, we have layers like the crust, mantle, outer core and
inner core. From the crust to the core, the density of the material increases.
What explains statement I. The semi-viscous and deformable nature of the asthenosphere provides a suitable surface
over which the rigid lithospheric plates can float and move gradually. This weak and ductile layer acts as a mechanical
base that facilitates the horizontal movement of plates, much like solid rafts drifting over a softer medium. Also the
lithospheric plates are less dense than the asthenosphere beneath them, which makes them float over the asthenosphere.
Therefore, the physical properties of the asthenosphere directly enable the mobility of lithospheric plates.
Knowledge Base:

[53]

questions.text: With reference to movement of the ocean water, consider the following statements: I. The time between the low tide and high tide, when the water level is rising, is called the ebb. II. The time between the high tide and low tide, when the water level is falling, is called the flow or flood. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Ocean tides represent the periodic rise and fall of sea levels, mainly caused by the gravitational pull of the Moon and
the Sun along with the Earth’s rotation. Within a tidal cycle, the terms ebb and flood (or flow), which denote the
direction of change in sea level.
Statement I is incorrect. The term ebb does not refer to the period when the water level rises from low tide to high tide.
In reality, ebb tide is the phase when the sea level is falling, that is, the period between high tide and the subsequent
low tide. During this stage, water gradually recedes from the shore as the tide moves outward.
Statement II is incorrect. The term flow or flood tide does not denote the period when water level falls from high tide to
low tide. Instead, flood tide refers to the phase when the sea level is rising, which occurs between low tide and the
following high tide. During this period, the seawater moves landward and gradually covers more of the coastal area.

questions.text: With reference to the Tropical Forest Forever Facility (TFFF), consider the following statements: I. It was launched at the 30th Conference of the Parties to UNFCCC, held in Belém, Brazil. II. India is a founding member and a primary financial contributor to the fund. III. It operates on a “payment-for- performance” mechanism, under which participating countries receive financial rewards based on verified preservation of tropical forests. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.

[54]

Statement I is correct: The Tropical Forests Forever Facility (TFFF) is a global climate-finance initiative formally
launched at the COP30 (30th Conference of the Parties to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change)
held in Belém, Brazil. The initiative was spearheaded by Brazil with the aim of mobilising long-term international
financial resources for the conservation of tropical forests and reducing deforestation.
Statement II is incorrect: India is not a founding member or primary financial contributor to the TFFF. Instead, India
has participated in the initiative in an observer capacity, supporting discussions on global forest conservation but not
formally joining as a founding funder.
Statement III is correct: The TFFF operates on a payment-for-performance (or results-based) model. It aims to raise
$125 billion through a "blended finance" structure ($25 billion from sponsors and $100 billion from private investors). The
returns from these investments are used to pay tropical forest countries an annual reward (estimated at $4 per
hectare) for every hectare of forest they preserve, provided their deforestation rate remains below 0.5%.
A mandatory requirement of the TFFF framework is that participating countries must allocate at least 20% of the
received funds to Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs). This recognizes their critical role as the
traditional guardians of tropical ecosystems.

questions.text: With reference to the environment, the term "Novel Entities" can be best described as:
questions.option_a: Naturally occurring biological species that evolve within ecosystems and help maintain ecological balance on the Earth.
questions.option_b: Newly evolved organisms that emerge through natural evolutionary adaptation to changing climatic conditions.
questions.option_c: Organic compounds produced by plants and microorganisms that support ecological interactions in ecosystems.
questions.option_d: Things created and introduced into the environment by human beings that could have positive or negative disruptive effects on the Earth’s system..
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
The term “Novel Entities” is used in the Planetary Boundaries framework, which identifies the environmental limits
within which humanity can safely operate in order to maintain the stability and resilience of the Earth system.
● Novel Entities refer to new substances, new forms of existing substances, or modified life forms introduced
into the environment through human activities that were previously absent in nature or existed only in very
small quantities. They may have potentially disruptive effects on ecological and Earth-system processes.
● These entities include synthetic chemicals such as industrial chemicals and pesticides, plastics and
microplastics, engineered nanomaterials, radioactive materials, and genetically modified organisms (GMOs).
● Many of these substances are highly persistent, widely dispersed, and capable of accumulating in ecosystems,
while natural environmental processes often lack the ability to effectively degrade or regulate them.
● As a result, their large-scale production and release can disrupt ecological processes and Earth system stability,
potentially causing long-term or uncertain impacts on ecosystems and human health.

questions.text: Consider the following statements regarding the Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025 in India: I. They constitute India’s first legally binding framework for reducing greenhouse gas emission intensity from industrial sectors. II. The rules will help operationalise the country’s domestic carbon market under the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS), 2023. III. The sectors such as fertiliser, thermal power generation, automobile manufacturing and food processing industries are the first four sectors to which these rules apply. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
The Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025 are a set of rules notified by the Government of India
under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 to mandate reductions in greenhouse gas emissions from energy-
intensive industries. They represent India’s first legally binding framework that sets sector-specific emission-intensity
reduction targets for industrial entities.
Statement I is correct: The Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025 (notified in October 2025)
represent India’s first legally binding framework to reduce emissions from the industrial sector.
Previously, schemes like the Perform, Achieve, and Trade (PAT) scheme focused on energy efficiency, but the GEI rules
directly mandate the reduction of carbon emission intensity.
The rules prescribe sector-specific greenhouse gas emission intensity targets, which are measured as emissions per
unit of industrial output. These emissions are quantified in tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent (tCO₂e) so that different
greenhouse gases can be measured under a common standard metric. The Rules set targets on greenhouse gas (GHG)
emissions per unit of product output—for example, the gases released in the production of a tonne of cement or
aluminium. Industries will earn carbon credits in lieu of meeting emissions targets, while those that fail to do so will have
to buy credits or pay environmental compensation.
Statement II is correct: These rules serve as the operational backbone for the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS),
2023.
While the CCTS established the market framework, the GEI Rules define the specific Compliance Mechanism. Industries
that over-perform (reduce emissions more than their target) earn Carbon Credit Certificates (CCCs), which they can sell
to under-performers in the domestic carbon market.
Statement III is incorrect: The initial phase of the GEI Target Rules applied only to a limited set of energy-intensive
industries, such as:
● Aluminium
● Cement
● Chlor-alkali
● Pulp and paper
Later, the notification, issued on 13.01.2026, brought Petroleum Refineries, Petrochemicals, Textiles and Secondary
Aluminium under the Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025.
The sectors mentioned; fertiliser, thermal power, automobile manufacturing, and food processing are not covered
under the initial notification of the GEI Target Rules.

questions.text: In the context of Biotechnology, the ‘GLP-1 receptor agonists’, recently mentioned in the news, are:
questions.option_a: A new class of drugs used for the treatment of Type-2 diabetes and obesity
questions.option_b: A class of antiviral drugs used for treating emerging zoonotic infections
questions.option_c: A group of monoclonal antibodies used in cancer immunotherapy
questions.option_d: A novel category of antibiotics used to combat antimicrobial resistance dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
GLP-1 receptor agonists (Glucagon-Like Peptide-1 receptor agonists) are synthetic drugs that mimic the action of the
natural hormone GLP-1, which is released in the intestine in response to food. They act on multiple pathways:

[56]

1. Stimulate insulin secretion – They increase insulin release from the pancreas when blood glucose levels are high.
2. Suppress glucagon secretion – This reduces the liver’s glucose output.
3. Slow gastric emptying – Food moves more slowly through the stomach, helping to control post-meal blood sugar
spikes.
4. Increase satiety – They reduce appetite, helping with weight management.
Because of these combined effects, GLP-1 receptor agonists are widely used in the management of type-2 diabetes and
have recently gained attention as effective weight-loss drugs for obesity management. Drugs like semaglutide,
dulaglutide and liraglutide belong to this class and have recently been in the news due to their expanding role in
metabolic health.

questions.text: In the context of biotechnology, which of the following best describes the term ‘Bio- hacking’?
questions.option_a: The use of genetically engineered microorganisms to produce medicines, vaccines, and industrial enzymes for large- scale commercial applications.
questions.option_b: The controlled manipulation of cellular processes in laboratories to develop new therapies for genetic disorders and chronic diseases.
questions.option_c: The development of artificial organs and bioengineered tissues through advanced regenerative medicine and tissue engineering techniques.
questions.option_d: The practice of optimizing or modifying one’s own biology through self- experimentation, lifestyle changes, supplements, and digital health technologies.
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Bio-hacking refers to a do-it-yourself (DIY) approach to modifying or optimising one’s own biology through self-
experimentation and data-driven lifestyle interventions aimed at improving health, performance, and longevity.
● It involves practices such as dietary changes, intermittent fasting, use of nutritional supplements, sleep
optimisation, and monitoring physiological parameters using wearable devices and digital health technologies.
● The concept has gained attention within the broader global longevity movement, which focuses on extending
healthy lifespan (healthspan) by making personalised adjustments to diet, metabolism, and daily habits based on
biological data.

questions.text: With reference to the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF), consider the following statements: I. It is an non-statutory executive body established by a resolution of the Government of India. II. It acts as an apex body to provide high- level strategic direction of scientific research in the country as per recommendations of the National Education Policy. III. The Union Minister of Science and Technology serves as the ex-officio President of the Governing Board of the ANRF. IV. It is funded exclusively through budgetary allocations from the Union Government. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) - established
through an Act of Parliament: ANRF Act, 2023, to provide high-level strategic directions for research, innovation, and
entrepreneurship in the fields of natural sciences, including mathematical sciences, engineering and technology,
environmental and earth sciences, health and agriculture, and scientific and technological interfaces of humanities and
social sciences. Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) has been established to promote research and
development and foster a culture of research and innovation throughout India’s Universities, Colleges, Research
Institutions, and R&D laboratories. ANRF acts as an apex body to provide high-level strategic direction of scientific
research in the country as per recommendations of the National Education Policy. ANRF forges collaborations among
the industry, academia, research institutions and government departments.
The ANRF has subsumed the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), which earlier functioned as a statutory
body under the Department of Science and Technology to fund scientific research. With the creation of ANRF, SERB has
been integrated into the new institutional framework.
The SERB Act, 2008 has been repealed, and its functions related to funding and promoting scientific research have been
integrated into the broader institutional framework of the ANRF.
Statement III is incorrect: The Prime Minister of India serves as the ex-officio President of the Governing Board of the
ANRF. The Union Minister of Science and Technology and the Union Minister of Education serve as the Vice-Presidents.
Statement IV is incorrect: The ANRF is not funded exclusively through budgetary allocations from the Union
Government. The Foundation aims to mobilize ₹50,000 crore during 2023–28 through various funding streams.
Out of this, ₹14,000 crore is to be provided by the Central Government, while the remaining amount will be mobilized
from other sources, including public sector enterprises, the private sector, philanthropic organizations, foundations, and
international bodies.
Hence, its funding structure is designed to combine government support with contributions from non-government
sources.

questions.text: With reference to the Union Budget of India, consider the following receipts: I. Recoveries of loans granted by Central Government to State and Union Territory Governments II. Proceeds from disinvestment of Central Government equity in Public Sector Undertakings. III. Fees and fines collected by the Central Government. IV. Dividends received by the Central Government from Public Sector Undertakings. V. Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections. How many of the above are treated as Capital Receipts of the Government of India?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
In government budgeting, receipts are broadly classified into Revenue Receipts and Capital Receipts.

[58]

Revenue Receipts are those which neither create a liability nor reduce government assets. These include tax revenue
receipts (like GST) and non-tax revenue receipts (like dividends, fees, and interest).
Capital Receipts, on the other hand, are either:
1. Create a liability for the government (such as borrowings), or
2. Lead to a reduction in government assets (such as disinvestment or recovery of loans).
Option I is correct. When the government recovers a loan earlier extended, it receives money but simultaneously its
asset (loan outstanding) reduces. Since it results in a reduction of an asset, it is classified as a capital receipt.
Option II is correct. Disinvestment involves selling government ownership in Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs). This
reduces government assets and brings in funds, thus qualifying as a capital receipt.
Option III is incorrect. Fees and fines are examples of Administrative Revenue, which fall under Non-Tax Revenue. They
are recurring receipts arising from government services or legal enforcement and do not involve asset sale or borrowing.
Option IV is incorrect. Dividends received from Public Sector Undertakings are part of Non-Tax Revenue, which falls
under Revenue Receipts. They represent income earned from government ownership but do not reduce ownership itself.
Option V is incorrect. GST is a Tax Revenue and therefore a Revenue Receipt. It does not create any liability nor does it
involve sale of assets.
Knowledge Base:
Source: https://egyankosh.ac.in/bitstream/123456789/84883/3/Unit-22.pdf UNIT 22 - Fiscal Policy - Public Revenue –

questions.text: With reference to 6G (Sixth Generation) wireless communication technology, consider the following statements: I. Compared to 5G, it will be using lower radio frequencies which will enable it to handle vast data at once. II. Bharat 6G Alliance is a multi-ministerial convergence initiative aimed to drive indigenous 6G research and development in India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.option_d: Neither I nor II dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
6G refers to the sixth generation of mobile wireless communication technology, which is expected to succeed 5G
networks in the coming decade (around 2030). It aims to provide extremely high data speeds, ultra-low latency, massive
connectivity and intelligent network management, enabling advanced digital applications beyond the capabilities of
current mobile networks.
Statement I is incorrect: 6G (Sixth Generation) wireless communication technology is expected to operate in extremely
high-frequency spectrum bands, particularly the terahertz (THz) band, generally considered to lie roughly between
100GHz to 10THz. These frequencies are higher than the millimetre-wave bands used in 5G, and they provide extremely
large bandwidth, which can support ultra-high data transmission capacities.
Thus, it will use higher radio frequencies than 5G, letting it handle vast data at once with almost no delay.
Statement II is incorrect: The Bharat 6G Alliance (B6GA) is an initiative of Indian industry, Telecom Service providers,
academia, national research institutions, and standards organizations. Supported by the government and industry, it
drives indigenous 6G research and development to create a self-reliant, globally competitive ecosystem for advanced
communications technologies.
Thus, it is not a multi-ministerial convergence initiative.
It was launched by the Honorable Minister of Communications as an initiative aimed at facilitating the realization of the
“Bharat 6G Vision“ for fostering the development of 6G technology in the country. It is an industry-led body, facilitated by
the government, that provides a collaborative platform to various technology stakeholders comprising public/private
companies, academia, research institutions, and Standard Development organizations.

questions.text: With reference to Web3 technology and its ecosystem, consider the following statements: I. Unlike Web 2.0 where data is stored in centralized repositories, Web 3.0 enables data to be stored in multiple locations simultaneously through decentralized data networks. II. Technologies such as decentralized finance (DeFi), non-fungible tokens (NFTs) and decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) form part of the emerging Web3 ecosystem. III. It can be used by credit-scoring agencies to share information about a customer with lenders without revealing the customer’s personal financial information. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Web3, represents the next stage in the evolution of the internet, characterized by decentralization, user ownership of
data, and greater use of advanced digital technologies.
● Unlike the earlier read-only internet (Web 1.0) and the interactive but centralized internet (Web 2.0) dominated by
large platforms, Web 3.0 aims to create a more open, transparent, and user-controlled digital ecosystem.
● It is primarily built on technologies such as Blockchain, Smart Contracts, and Artificial Intelligence, which enable
decentralized applications, secure peer-to-peer transactions, and intelligent data processing.
● In this model, users can interact directly through decentralized networks without relying heavily on centralized
intermediaries.
Statement I is correct: In the Web 2.0 model, data is largely stored and controlled by centralized platforms such as
large technology companies that maintain centralized servers or cloud infrastructures.
In contrast, Web 3.0 is based on decentralization, where data and applications are distributed across a network of
computers (nodes) using technologies like blockchain and distributed storage systems. This allows data to be stored
and verified across multiple locations simultaneously, reducing dependence on centralized intermediaries and increasing
resilience and transparency.
Statement II is correct: The Web3 ecosystem includes several emerging technological frameworks built on blockchain
networks. These include:
● Decentralized Finance (DeFi), which provides financial services such as lending, borrowing, and trading without
traditional intermediaries like banks.

[60]

● Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs), which represent unique digital ownership of assets such as art, music, or virtual items
using blockchain verification.
● Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs), which enable organizations to be governed collectively by
participants through rules encoded in smart contracts rather than centralized authorities.
These technologies together represent the broader decentralized digital economy emerging within Web3.
Statement III is correct: Web3 tools can help authenticate AI models using zero-knowledge proof (ZKP). Blockchain has
a cryptographic technique called ZKP that allows one party to prove the validity of a statement to another party without
revealing any specific information about that statement itself. Using this method, credit-scoring firms, for instance, can
share information on users with lenders without revealing their personal financial information. And healthcare-
related firms can share information about patients without disclosing specific medical reports. ZKP can assuage security
concerns arising from use of data in Large Language Models.

questions.text: With reference to CRISPR–Cas9, consider the following statements: I. It is a gene-editing technology derived from the natural immune defense mechanism of bacteria. II. Lyfgenia is the world’s first approved CRISPR-Cas9 gene-edited cell therapy for the treatment of sickle cell disease. III. The use of CRISPR-Cas9 technology in human embryos could enable the selection or enhancement of genetic traits in future children, raising the possibility of “designer babies”. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: CRISPR-Cas9 technology is derived from the natural adaptive immune system of bacteria and
archaea.
● In this system, microbes capture fragments of viral DNA and store them in clustered DNA sequences known as
CRISPR.
● When the virus attacks again, RNA molecules guide Cas proteins to recognize and cut the invading viral DNA.

[61]

● Scientists adapted this biological defense mechanism into a powerful genome-editing tool that allows targeted
modification of genes in plants, animals and humans.
In CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing, a guide RNA (gRNA) is designed to match a specific DNA sequence in the genome. This
RNA directs the Cas9 enzyme, which acts like molecular scissors, to that precise location in the DNA. Cas9 then creates a
double-strand break in the DNA. During the natural DNA-repair process, scientists can introduce insertions, deletions, or
corrections of genes, enabling targeted genetic modification.
Statement II is incorrect: Lyfgenia is a gene-addition therapy, not a CRISPR-Cas9 gene-editing therapy. It uses a
lentiviral vector to introduce a modified functional hemoglobin gene into a patient’s stem cells to treat Sickle Cell Disease.
The first approved therapy that uses CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing for treating sickle cell disease is Casgevy, which edits a
patient’s hematopoietic stem cells to increase production of fetal hemoglobin, thereby reducing disease symptoms.
Statement III is correct: The application of CRISPR technology to human embryos (germline editing) raises significant
ethical concerns.
● Since changes made in embryos can be heritable, the technology could potentially be used to select or enhance
genetic traits such as physical characteristics or intelligence.
● This possibility has led to concerns about the creation of “designer babies,” along with issues of safety, unintended
mutations, inequality in access, and long-term societal implications.

questions.text: With reference to recent space missions, consider the following pairs: Space Mission Description/ Objective I. TRACERS Earth observation mission to monitor small changes in land surface, glaciers and ecosystems using dual- frequency radar. II. Europa Clipper Mission designed to land a rover on Jupiter's moon to drill through its icy crust and search for life. III. PLATO Mission aimed at detecting Earth-like exoplanets around bright stars and studying their internal structure. IV. Proba-3 Mission using two satellites flying in formation to create artificial solar eclipses for studying the Sun’s corona. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
Pair I is incorrect: TRACERS
TRACERS (Tandem Reconnection and Cusp Electrodynamics Reconnaissance Satellites) is a NASA mission consisting of
two satellites designed to study magnetic reconnection and the interaction between the solar wind and Earth’s
magnetosphere, particularly in the polar cusp region.
The description given in the pair refers to an Earth-observation radar mission monitoring land surface deformation,
glaciers, and ecosystems, which actually corresponds to NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR), a joint mission
of NASA and Indian Space Research Organisation that uses dual-frequency synthetic aperture radar.
Pair II is incorrect: Europa Clipper
Europa Clipper is a mission of NASA intended to investigate the habitability of Europa, a moon of Jupiter.
The spacecraft will orbit Jupiter and perform multiple close flybys of Europa, studying its ice shell, subsurface ocean,
surface composition, and geological activity.
However, the mission does not involve landing a rover or drilling through the icy crust. It is designed as an
orbiter/flyby reconnaissance mission, not a lander mission.
Pair III is correct: PLATO
PLATO (PLAnetary Transits and Oscillations of stars) is a European Space Agency mission scheduled for launch in late
2026. It is intended to detect Earth-like exoplanets orbiting bright stars and study the internal structure of their host
stars. It will use the transit method and stellar oscillation measurements to determine planetary sizes, ages and
characteristics of planetary systems.
Pair IV is correct: Proba-3

[62]

Proba-3 is a European Space Agency mission consisting of two satellites flying in precise formation—one acting as an
occulter to block the Sun and the other carrying a coronagraph. This configuration creates artificial solar eclipses in
space, allowing scientists to study the Sun’s corona and solar activity with unprecedented precision.

questions.text: The terms ‘O3b mPower’, ‘Global Xpress (GX)’ and ‘EchoStar’, often mentioned in the news, are primarily related to-
questions.option_a: Deep-space exploration missions for studying outer planets.
questions.option_c: Global satellite communication and broadband internet systems.
questions.option_d: Space-based solar power generation projects. dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
O3b mPOWER, Global Xpress (GX) and EchoStar are satellite-based communication and broadband connectivity
systems that provide high-speed internet and data services across the globe.
1. O3b mPOWER:
● It is a next-generation medium Earth orbit (MEO) satellite constellation that provides high-throughput, low-
latency broadband connectivity to telecom operators, governments, maritime and aviation sectors.
2. Global Xpress (GX):
● It is a Ka-band geostationary satellite communication network developed by Inmarsat that delivers high-speed
global broadband services for maritime, aviation, government and commercial users.
3. EchoStar:
● It is a satellite communications company that operates communication satellites and broadband systems
(including those used for HughesNet services) providing satellite internet and broadcasting services worldwide.
Therefore: All three are commercial satellite communication systems designed to deliver global broadband and
connectivity services.

questions.text: With reference to the S-400 Triumf Air Defence System, often referred to in India as the “Sudarshan Chakra”, consider the following statements: I. It is a surface-to-air missile system capable of engaging aerial targets at ranges up to about 400 km. II. It is a joint air-defence system developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) of India and Rosoboronexport of Russia. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: The S-400 Triumf is a long-range surface-to-air missile defence system. Its longest-range
interceptor missile (40N6) can destroy aerial targets at distances of up to about 400 km. This makes it one of the most
advanced long-range air-defence systems in the world.

[63]

The S-400 has multi-target engagement capability. It can track a large number of aerial objects simultaneously and
engage multiple threats at the same time, including fighter aircraft, cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, and unmanned
aerial vehicles (UAVs).
Statement II is incorrect: The system is not jointly developed by India and Russia. It was developed by the Russian
defence company Almaz-Antey and exported through Rosoboronexport. India has procured the system from Russia, but
it was not developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).

questions.text: With reference to Launch Vehicle Mark- 3 (LVM3) of the Indian Space Research Organisation, which of the following statements are correct? I. It can place about 4 tonnes of payload into Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit and about 8 tonnes of payload into Low Earth Orbit. II. It has a two-stage configuration consisting of solid boosters and a cryogenic upper stage. III. The Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3 missions were launched using the LVM3 launch vehicle. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM3), earlier known as GSLV Mk-III, is India’s heaviest operational
launch vehicle, designed to launch heavy communication satellites into Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit and to
undertake complex missions such as lunar and deep-space missions.
LVM3 has a payload capacity of about 4 tonnes to Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO) and around 8 tonnes to Low
Earth Orbit (LEO), making it suitable for launching heavier satellites and complex missions.
Statement II is incorrect: LVM3 does not have a two-stage configuration. It is a three-stage launch vehicle consisting
of:
● Two solid strap-on boosters (S200)
● A liquid core stage (L110)
● A cryogenic upper stage (C25) powered by the CE-20 cryogenic engine.
Statement III is correct: Major lunar missions of India, including Chandrayaan-2 (2019) and Chandrayaan-3 (2023), were
launched using the LVM3 launch vehicle.

questions.text: With reference to Green Methanol, consider the following statements: I. It can be produced by synthesizing green hydrogen with carbon dioxide captured through Carbon Capture and Utilisation (CCU). II. Its use as a fuel can significantly reduce emissions of sulphur oxides (SOx), nitrogen oxides (NOx) and particulate matter compared to conventional fossil fuels. III. Its combustion produces only water vapour and does not release any carbon dioxide. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct: Green Methanol is produced by synthesizing green hydrogen (hydrogen generated through
electrolysis of water using renewable energy such as solar or wind) with captured carbon dioxide (CO₂) through Carbon
Capture and Utilisation (CCU).

[64]

● The captured CO₂ acts as the carbon source and reacts with hydrogen in a catalytic process to form methanol
(CH₃OH). Since renewable energy and recycled CO₂ are used, the fuel is considered low-carbon or carbon-neutral in
lifecycle terms.
Statement II is correct: Methanol can be used as a fuel in internal combustion (IC) engines. It can either be used
directly with certain engine modifications or blended with conventional fuels such as gasoline. Because of its high
octane number and clean-burning characteristics, it is also being explored as an alternative fuel in sectors such as road
transport and shipping.
Compared with many conventional fossil fuels such as diesel or heavy fuel oil, the combustion of methanol generally
produces lower emissions of sulphur oxides (SOx), nitrogen oxides (NOx) and particulate matter. This is mainly because
methanol contains no sulphur and burns more cleanly, making it a relatively cleaner fuel option.
Statement III is incorrect: Although methanol burns relatively cleanly, its combustion does not produce only water
vapour. It also produces carbon dioxide (CO₂) and small amounts of other compounds such as formaldehyde.
However, when green methanol is produced using captured CO₂, the overall carbon cycle can be close to carbon-neutral,
but the combustion process itself still releases CO₂.

questions.text: With reference to the Urban Challenge Fund (UCF), recently mentioned in news, consider the following statements: I. Under the Fund, Central assistance will cover 25% of the project cost, subject to raising a minimum 50% of the project cost from the market sources. II. In order to facilitate first-time access to market finance for certain Cities/Urban Local Bodies, a Credit Repayment Guarantee Scheme of ₹5,000 crore has been approved. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
Statement I is correct. The Union Cabinet has approved the launch of the Urban Challenge Fund (UCF) with a total
Central Assistance (CA) of Rs. One lakh crore. CA will cover 25% of the project cost, subject to raising a minimum 50% of
the project cost from the market. A minimum of 50 per cent of project financing has to be mobilised from market
sources, including municipal bonds, bank loans and Public–Private Partnerships (PPPs). The remaining share may be
contributed by States, Union Territories (UTs), Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) or other sources.This will lead to a total
investment of Rs. Four lakh crore in the urban sector in next five years, marking a paradigm shift in India’s urban
development approach from grant- based financing to market-linked, reform-driven and outcome-oriented
infrastructure creation.
The Urban Challenge Fund will leverage market finance, private participation and citizen- centric reforms for delivery of
high-quality urban infrastructure. The Fund aims to build resilient, productive, inclusive and climate-responsive cities,
positioning those as key drivers of the country’s next phase of economic growth.
The scheme primarily covers three categories of cities:
1. All cities with a population of 10 lakh or more (based on 2025 estimates).
2. All State and Union Territory capitals that are not already included in the first category.
3. Major industrial cities with a population of 1 lakh or more.
Statement II is correct. A major challenge faced by many smaller cities in India is their limited ability to raise funds from
financial markets. Urban Local Bodies in such cities often lack the creditworthiness required to access bank loans or
issue municipal bonds.
To address this problem, in order to facilitate first-time access to market finance for all Cities/ULBs in Northeastern &
Hilly States/UTs and smaller ULBs (<1,00,000 population) in other States/UTs, a Credit Repayment Guarantee Scheme

[65]

of ₹5,000 crore has been approved under Urban Challenge Fund. The scheme will provide a Central guarantee of up to ₹7
crore or 70 per cent of the loan amount (whichever is lower) for first-time loans. On successful repayment of the first
loan, central guarantee of ₹7 crore or 50 per cent of the loan amount (whichever is lower) will be provided. This will
effectively support projects of ₹20 crore for the first time and ₹28 crore for subsequent projects in smaller cities.

questions.text: Consider the following statements: The Inflation-Indexed Bonds (IIBs) in India provides protection on its: I. Principal payment II. Interest payment Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.option_d: Neither I nor II dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer
Inflation steadily reduces the purchasing power of money. When prices rise, the real return from traditional fixed-
income instruments declines because the interest earned may not keep pace with inflation. To address this issue,
governments issue Inflation-Indexed Bonds (IIBs).
These bonds are designed to protect investors against inflation risk by linking the value of the investment to an inflation
index. In India, the Reserve Bank of India introduced Inflation-Indexed Bonds as part of the government securities
programme to provide investors with an instrument that safeguards the real value of savings.
Statement I is correct. Inflation-Indexed Bonds are structured so that the principal value of the bond is linked to an
inflation index.
This linkage is achieved through an index ratio, which adjusts the original face value of the bond according to changes in
the inflation index over time. When inflation rises, the adjusted principal increases, thereby preserving the purchasing
power of the invested amount.
Statement II is correct: Inflation-Indexed Bonds also protect investors through their interest payment mechanism.
These bonds carry a fixed real coupon rate, but the interest is calculated on the inflation-adjusted principal rather than
the original face value. As inflation increases and the principal is revised upward, the interest payments also increase
proportionately. This feature ensures that investors receive returns that keep pace with inflation, thereby maintaining
the real yield on the investment.

questions.text: In the context of the Indian economy, which of the following best describes the term “Crowding Out Effect”?
questions.option_a: A situation where private sector investment increases due to a fall in government expenditure.
questions.option_b: A situation where government spending directly increases private investment through subsidies.
questions.option_c: A policy in which the government reduces taxes to stimulate private consumption and investment.
questions.option_d: A situation where increased borrowing by the government from the market decreases the quantity of funds available for private sector investment.
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Governments frequently rely on fiscal policy i.e. changes in public expenditure and taxation, to influence economic
activity. When the government increases spending, it aims to stimulate demand and raise national income. However, such
policies may sometimes produce an unintended side effect known as the Crowding Out Effect, which explains how
government borrowing can influence private sector investment decisions.
The Crowding Out Effect occurs when higher government spending leads to increased borrowing from financial
markets, which raises the overall demand for funds in the economy. This refers to a phenomenon where increased

[66]

borrowing by the government to meet its spending needs causes a decrease in the quantity of funds that is available to
meet the investment needs of the private sector.
As the demand for funds rises, interest rates tend to increase. Higher interest rates make borrowing more expensive for
businesses. Consequently, firms may postpone or reduce their private investment in projects such as factories,
machinery or infrastructure. Thus, government borrowing “crowds out” private investment, meaning that part of the
economic stimulus created by public spending is offset by a decline in private sector investment.

questions.text: If the ratio of manufacturing to agriculture sector deflators is greater than 1 in an economy, it indicates:
questions.option_a: Manufacturing prices are rising more rapidly relative to agricultural prices and better Terms of Trade for the manufacturing sector.
questions.option_b: Agricultural prices are rising more rapidly relative to Manufacturing prices and better Terms of Trade for the manufacturing sector.
questions.option_c: Manufacturing prices are rising more rapidly relative to agricultural prices and better Terms of Trade for the agricultural sector.
questions.option_d: Agricultural prices are rising more rapidly relative to Manufacturing prices and better Terms of Trade for the agricultural sector.
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer
The GDP deflator is a broad measure of general price level in an economy. It is defined as the ratio of nominal GDP to
real GDP.
The GDP deflator of a sector is the ratio of its Nominal (current price) GVA to Real (constant price) GVA, and acts as a
price index with the base year value equal to 1. It reflects how prices in that sector change over time.
The ratio of manufacturing to agriculture sector deflators tells how manufacturing prices have moved relative to
agricultural prices. If the ratio is greater than 1, then Manufacturing prices are higher relative to agricultural prices,
which implies better terms of trade for manufacturing and vice versa.

questions.text: With reference to healthcare financing in India, consider the following statements: Statement I: India has one of the highest Out- of-Pocket Expenditure (OOPE) as a percentage of Total Health Expenditure (THE). Statement II: Public expenditure on healthcare in India has generally remained below 2% of GDP, which is considered low by global standards. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I.
questions.option_b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I.
questions.option_c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.
questions.option_d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct.
questions.explanation:
Option a is the correct answer.
Financing healthcare is a crucial issue for developing economies like India. The structure of health expenditure
determines how much financial burden falls directly on individuals and households. In many countries with strong
public health systems, governments bear a significant share of healthcare costs. However, in countries where public
spending on health is relatively low, individuals often have to finance treatment from their own savings.
Statement I is correct. India experiences a relatively high level of Out-of-Pocket Expenditure (OOPE) on healthcare.
OOPE refers to the amount that households directly pay from their own resources for medical services such as
consultation, diagnostics, medicines, hospitalisation and follow-up care.
In India, a substantial proportion of total health expenditure comes directly from households. As per National Health
Accounts Estimates 2021-22, the Out-of-Pocket Expenditure (OOPE) as a percentage of Total Health Expenditure (THE) is
39.4%. OOPE on health as a percentage of THE in the country for the year 2017-18, 2018-19, 2019-20, 2020-21, and 2021-22
are 48.8%, 48.2%, 47.1%, 44.4%, and 39.4% respectively and therefore there is a declining trend in OOPE as a percentage of
THE. This means that many families must rely on personal savings or borrow money to meet medical expenses, which
can significantly affect their financial stability. Studies and policy reports have repeatedly shown that medical spending
can push a large number of people into poverty each year.
Statement II is correct. Historically, government spending on health in India has remained relatively low as a share of
GDP, generally hovering around 1–1.5% of GDP for a long time.

[67]

Compared with many other countries, especially developed economies and several emerging economies, this level of
public expenditure is considered quite modest. Lower public spending implies fewer publicly funded hospitals, limited
free treatment options and reduced coverage of health services through government programmes.
Statement II explains Statement I. When government spending on healthcare is limited, the public health system
cannot fully meet the medical needs of the population. As a result, individuals often turn to private healthcare providers
for treatment. Private healthcare typically requires direct payment by patients, which increases the share of household
spending in total health expenditure. Therefore, low public health expenditure contributes significantly to high Out-of-
Pocket Expenditure in India.

questions.text: Consider the following states: I. Andhra Pradesh II. Telangana III. Karnataka IV. Gujarat V. Maharashtra How many of the above States in India currently have a bicameral legislature?
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
The structure of legislatures in India has its historical roots in the constitutional reforms introduced during British
rule. In the early twentieth century, the British administration recognised the need to provide Indians with greater
participation in governance and law-making. As a result, the Montagu–Chelmsford Reforms led to the enactment of the
Government of India Act, 1919, which introduced bicameral legislatures at the Centre and in certain provinces.
Independent India largely adopted this parliamentary model. However, the Constitution under Article 169 allows states to
have either unicameral or bicameral legislatures. While Parliament is permanently bicameral, states have the flexibility
to decide whether to establish a Legislative Council.
Option I is correct. Andhra Pradesh has a bicameral legislature, consisting of the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha)
and the Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad). The state re-established its Legislative Council in 2007 after it had been
abolished earlier.
Option II is correct. Telangana, formed in 2014, also has a bicameral legislature. The state inherited the Legislative
Council structure from the erstwhile unified Andhra Pradesh and continues to function with both houses of the
legislature.
Option III is correct. Karnataka is the state with a bicameral legislature. It has a Legislative Council that functions as
the upper chamber, reviewing and revising legislation passed by the Legislative Assembly.
Option IV is incorrect. Gujarat does not have a Legislative Council. Its legislature consists of only one house i.e. the
Legislative Assembly, making it a unicameral state legislature.
Option V is correct. Maharashtra is among the states that follow the bicameral legislative system. Its legislature consists
of the Maharashtra Legislative Assembly and the Maharashtra Legislative Council, making it one of the 6 states in India
with two legislative chambers.
Knowledge Base:
● There are Six states with Bicameral Legislative system in India: Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka, Maharashtra,
Bihar and Uttar Pradesh,

questions.text: With reference to Contempt of Court in India, which of the following statements is/are correct? I. The Constitution of India explicitly defines civil contempt and criminal contempt of court and prescribes the punishment for both. II. Civil contempt includes wilful disobedience of a court’s judgment, decree, direction, order, writ or breach of an undertaking given to the court. III. Fair and accurate reporting of judicial proceedings and reasonable criticism of a judgment after the case is decided do not amount to contempt of court. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.option_d: I, II and III dmissions@forumias.academy | PTS 2026 | Test Code:
questions.explanation:
Option c is the correct answer.
In India, contempt powers serve to protect the administration of justice, but they operate within a constitutional
framework that also respects freedom of speech under Article 19(1)a). The balance between judicial authority and
democratic criticism is maintained through constitutional provisions and the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971.
Statement I is incorrect. The Constitution of India does not define civil contempt or criminal contempt. Instead, these
definitions are provided under Section 2 of the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971, which was enacted by Parliament following
the recommendations of the H. N. Sanyal Committee.
Statement II is correct. Under the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971, civil contempt refers to wilful disobedience of a court’s
judgment, decree, direction, order, writ or other process, or the wilful breach of an undertaking given to the court.
This form of contempt is primarily concerned with ensuring compliance with court orders, thereby safeguarding the
effectiveness of the judicial process.
Statement III is correct. Not every comment about the judiciary amounts to contempt. The law recognises certain
important safeguards for freedom of expression. For instance, innocent publication, fair and accurate reporting of
judicial proceedings, and fair criticism of a judicial decision after the case has been decided are not treated as
contempt. These protections ensure that judicial accountability and public debate remain possible in a democratic
society, while still preserving the authority of the courts.
Knowledge Base: The Supreme Court’s power to punish for contempt originates from constitutional provisions. Article
129 declares the Supreme Court to be a Court of Record, which includes the inherent power to punish for contempt of
itself. Additionally, Article 142(2) empowers the Supreme Court to investigate and punish any person for contempt of the
Court. These provisions ensure that the apex court can protect its authority and ensure compliance with its orders.

questions.text: Consider the following information: I. Armed gangs control nearly 80–90% of the capital city and key access routes. II. The country functions as a transshipment hub for cocaine and cannabis moving from Latin America to North America and Europe. III. The country has not held general elections for nearly a decade and is currently governed by a transitional council tasked with conducting elections. Which one of the following countries is best described in the information given above?
questions.explanation:
Option b is the correct answer.
In recent years, Haiti has been in the international spotlight due to rising gang violence, political instability and a
prolonged governance crisis. Weak law-enforcement institutions and the absence of a stable elected government have
allowed organised criminal groups to gain significant influence, particularly in and around the capital city.
Statement I. In the capital city of Haiti Port-au-Prince, armed gangs are estimated to control nearly 80–90 per cent of
the city. They dominate major roads and entry points, which allows them to regulate the movement of goods and people.
Through extortion, kidnappings and control of transport routes, these gangs have significantly weakened the authority of
the state in the capital region.
Statement II. Due to its strategic location in the Caribbean and its long coastline with limited surveillance, Haiti has
increasingly become a transshipment point for drugs such as cocaine and cannabis. These drugs typically originate in
Latin American production regions and are transported through the Caribbean towards North American and European
markets.

[69]

Statement III. Haiti has not held national elections for several years, largely because of persistent insecurity and
institutional instability. In response to the political vacuum, a transitional presidential council has been formed to
restore order, prepare for elections and re-establish democratic governance. However, the process remains challenging
due to continued violence and fragile state institutions.

questions.text: With reference to the Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) in India, consider the following statements: I. It functions under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. II. It is responsible for appraisal of activities involving large scale use of hazardous microorganisms and recombinants in research and industrial production from the environmental angle. III. It is responsible for appraisal of proposals relating to release of genetically engineered (GE) organisms and products into the environment including experimental field trials. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
Statements I, II and III are correct: The Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) functions in the Ministry of
Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC). As per Rules, 1989, it is responsible for appraisal of activities
involving large scale use of hazardous microorganisms and recombinants in research and industrial production from
the environmental angle. The committee is also responsible for appraisal of proposals relating to release of genetically
engineered (GE) organisms and products into the environment including experimental field trials.

questions.text: With reference to the Comptroller and Auditor-General (CAG) of India, which of the following statements are correct? I. S/he is appointed by the President of India by warrant under his/her hand and seal. II. S/he shall not be eligible for further office either under the Government of India or under the Government of any State after s/he has ceased to hold the office. III. The administrative expenses of the office of the CAG are charged upon the Consolidated Fund of India. IV. S/he is empowered to audit the accounts of the Union, the States and other authorities or bodies as prescribed by Parliament by law. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.option_d: I, II, III and IV dmissions@forumias.academy |
questions.explanation:
Option d is the correct answer.
The Comptroller and Auditor-General (CAG) of India is a key constitutional authority entrusted with ensuring financial
accountability and transparency in the functioning of the government. Established under Articles 148 to 151 of the
Constitution, the CAG acts as the guardian of the public purse by auditing government receipts and expenditures. Its
reports enable Parliament and State Legislatures to exercise effective oversight over the executive’s financial
administration.
Statement I is correct. According to Article 148 of the Constitution, There shall be a Comptroller and Auditor-General of
India who shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal and shall only be removed from office
in like manner and on the like grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court.
Statement II is correct. The Constitution explicitly states that the Comptroller and Auditor-General shall not be eligible
for further office either under the Government of India or under the Government of any State after he has ceased to hold
his office.
This restriction is meant to ensure that the CAG functions with complete neutrality and independence, without any
expectation of post-retirement appointments from the executive.

[70]

Statement III is correct. The administrative expenses of the office of the CAG, including salaries, allowances and
pensions of its staff, are charged upon the Consolidated Fund of India. Expenditure charged on the Consolidated Fund
does not require annual parliamentary approval, thereby ensuring the financial independence of the institution.
Statement IV is correct. Article 149 authorises the CAG to perform such duties and exercise such powers in relation to
the accounts of the Union, States and other authorities or bodies as prescribed by Parliament by law. In practice, this
allows the CAG to conduct various forms of audits, including financial audits, compliance audits and performance audits
of government programmes.
A recent example is the CAG performance audit of the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), where the
auditor examined the implementation of the skill development scheme and highlighted issues related to monitoring,
training outcomes and placement tracking. Such audits demonstrate how the CAG’s constitutional powers translate into
practical oversight of major public programmes
